❯  is Government of india any different ayanamsa                                                                                                                                                                              
● Yes and no — they're the same ayanamsa in principle, but there's a subtle precision distinction worth knowing.                                                     

  Government of India's official ayanamsa = Lahiri (Chitrapaksha)                                                                                                       
  History. The Indian Calendar Reform Committee (ICRC), chaired by astrophysicist Meghnad Saha, was appointed by the Government of India in 1952 and published its     report in 1955. The ICRC adopted N.C. Lahiri's ayanamsa as the official sidereal reference for the Rashtriya Panchang (India's national calendar) and the Indian     Ephemeris published by the Positional Astronomy Centre, Kolkata.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          So "Lahiri" and "Government of India ayanamsa" refer to the same ayanamsa system.                                                                                  

  Value: ~23°51' at J2000.0 (~23.85°).

  Called "Chitrapaksha" because it places the bright star Chitra (Spica, α Virginis) at approximately 180°00' sidereal longitude — right at the boundary of Kanni    
  (Virgo) and Thulam (Libra).

  The subtle distinction — Lahiri vs True Chithirai

  ┌────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────┐
  │         System         │             How Chitra is positioned              │          J2000 value           │ Swiss Eph constant │
  ├────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
  │ Lahiri (Govt of India) │ ~180° (formula-based; small drift over time)      │ 23°51'11"                      │ SIDM_LAHIRI        │
  ├────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
  │ True Chithirai         │ EXACTLY 180° 0' 0" (observation-locked, no drift) │ 23°51'48" (slightly different) │ SIDM_TRUE_CITRA    │
  └────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────┘

  The difference is ~30 arc-seconds — which is ~2 seconds of Moon's motion, ~30 minutes of Sun's motion, and negligible for practical natal astrology. But for       
  precise muhurta and degree-sensitive yogas, True Chithirai is stricter.

  Swiss Ephemeris has MULTIPLE Lahiri variants

  ┌───────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Variant      │                                                 Purpose                                                 │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ SIDM_LAHIRI       │ Standard Lahiri — Government of India default (this is what 99% of software uses when you say "Lahiri") │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ SIDM_LAHIRI_1940  │ Lahiri's ORIGINAL 1940 epoch (before ICRC standardization) — historical reconstruction only             │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ SIDM_LAHIRI_VP285 │ Refined Lahiri variant (technical/research)                                                             │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ SIDM_LAHIRI_ICRC  │ ICRC-committee-refined variant — slightly different from standard Lahiri, rarely used                   │
  └───────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  For practical purposes: "Government of India ayanamsa" = SIDM_LAHIRI (the standard Lahiri). Josi's current code (astrology_service.py:56) already uses this.       

  ---
  Back to the decision: this confirms that Lahiri as B2C default = Government of India ayanamsa — Josi's end-users see the same ayanamsa as Rashtriya Panchang, Drik 
  Panchang, Kalnirnay, Indian Ephemeris, and 90%+ of Indian panchangam publishers. No gap.

  Still waiting on your pick — Ayanamsa Q (A / B / C / D / E) from the previous question.

❯ what is difference between porphyry and bhava chalith and sripathi

● Good question — these three are closely related but distinct. Let me break it down.

  Porphyry, Sripati, Bhava Chalit — the relationships

  Porphyry (3rd century CE, Hellenistic)

  - Algorithm: Trisect each of the 4 ecliptic quadrants (Lagna→MC, MC→Desc, Desc→IC, IC→Lagna) into 3 equal arcs.
  - Each resulting cusp marks the START of a house.
  - Houses within the same quadrant are equal-sized; quadrants differ in total arc (depending on latitude and obliquity).
  - Polar-stable (works at any latitude).
  - Origin: Porphyry of Tyre, ~234-305 CE.

  Sripati (11th century CE, India)

  - Algorithm: Take Porphyry cusps, then take the MIDPOINTS between consecutive Porphyry cusps.
  - Sripati's key insight: the CENTER of a bhava is its strongest point (bhava-madhya), not the edge.
  - So in Sripati, the "cusp" is the middle of the Porphyry-calculated house, and the house extends from halfway to the previous bhava-madhya to halfway to the next.
  - Origin: Formalized in Sripati Paddhati (11th c, India) by the astrologer Sripati.
  - Parashari-specific — classical text (Parasara/Sripati lineage) uses this for bhava-differentiated chart evaluation.

  Bhava Chalit (the CONCEPT, not an algorithm)

  - Bhava Chalit = "the bhava chart" — a separate, second chart computed alongside the primary rasi chart.
  - It shows which bhava each planet actually occupies based on geometric cusp computation, as opposed to which rasi it sits in.
  - The underlying algorithm for bhava chalit is TYPICALLY Sripati in Parashari practice — but historically, some lineages have used Porphyry, Kashinath, or even    
  Placidus for the bhava-chalit computation.
  - So "Bhava Chalit" is the usage pattern (dual-chart Parashari evaluation), and Sripati is the algorithm most Parashari schools use to compute it.

  Visual relationship

  Ecliptic quadrant: Lagna (0°) ───── MC (90°)  [example quadrant]

  Porphyry cusps at: 0°, 30°, 60°, 90°  (trisected; each cusp = HOUSE START)

  Sripati cusps at:  0°, 15°, 45°, 75°, 90°  (midpoints of Porphyry pairs;
                                              each cusp = BHAVA-MADHYA / house MIDDLE)

  So a planet at 20° ecliptic longitude:
    - Porphyry:  in house 1 (0°-30°)
    - Sripati:   in bhava 1 (center at 15°, extends from 0° to 45°-midpoint = 22.5°)

  A planet at 25°:
    - Porphyry:  still in house 1 (0°-30°)
    - Sripati:   in bhava 2 (center at 45°, extends from 22.5° to 60°)

  Use cases — where each is used

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                           Chart / Purpose                            │                                   Convention                                    │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Rasi chart (sign-lordship, aspects, yogas)                           │ Whole Sign — planets in their sign; Lagna-sign = house 1 entirely               │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Bhava chart / Bhava Chalit (which bhava-matter is actually affected) │ Sripati — evaluates the planet's actual bhava position based on geometric cusps │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Western natal chart                                                  │ Single system — usually Placidus, sometimes Porphyry or Koch                    │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Hellenistic/Medieval horary                                          │ Porphyry (original), Regiomontanus, Alcabitius                                  │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Key Parashari insight

  In Parashari practice, you don't pick one house system — you compute two charts:

  1. Rasi chart (Whole Sign): planet in Leo sign = planet in Leo-rasi. Used for sign-based analysis — planetary friendships, exaltations, aspects to signs,
  rasi-based yogas.
  2. Bhava Chalit chart (Sripati): planet in Leo rasi might be in 5th bhava OR 4th bhava OR 6th bhava — depending on where Lagna-degree falls in Leo. Used to assess 
  WHICH life-matter the planet actually influences. If Lagna is at 28° Leo and planet is at 5° Leo, the planet is technically in Leo rasi but in the 12th bhava      
  (house of loss/isolation) — not the 1st bhava.

  This dual-chart method is uniquely Parashari. Western astrology uses ONE house system. That's why Parashari software needs BOTH Whole Sign (for rasi) AND Sripati  
  (for bhava chalit) computed simultaneously — and why Josi's proposed B2C default is "Whole Sign + Bhava Chalit/Sripati auto-computed as secondary panel."

  Summary

  ┌────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                        │              Porphyry               │           Sripati           │               Bhava Chalit               │
  ├────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ What is it?            │ House system (algorithm)            │ House system (algorithm)    │ Chart concept (usage pattern)            │
  ├────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Cusps mark...          │ House START                         │ Bhava MIDDLE (bhava-madhya) │ N/A — it's a chart, not a cusp method    │
  ├────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Origin                 │ Hellenistic (3rd c CE)              │ Parashari (11th c CE India) │ Parashari classical dual-chart technique │
  ├────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Used by                │ Hellenistic natal, some Vedic bhava │ Parashari bhava chart       │ Parashari dual-chart method              │
  ├────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Relationship to others │ Base for Sripati                    │ Midpoint of Porphyry cusps  │ Usually computed via Sripati             │
  └────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Simplest mental model: Porphyry is a Hellenistic house system. Sripati is the Parashari adaptation of Porphyry (shifts cusps by half a house). Bhava Chalit is the 
  Parashari practice of computing a secondary chart using Sripati alongside the Whole Sign rasi chart.

  ---
  Back to the decision — which option (A / B / C / D / E) for House System?

  Given this clarification, Option A's proposal (Whole Sign B2C default + Bhava Chalit/Sripati auto-computed secondary) is the classically correct Parashari
  dual-chart approach.

✻ Cogitated for 51s

❯ in porphyry

● Interpreting as "tell me more about Porphyry specifically." If you meant something else, redirect me.

  Porphyry house system — deeper dive

  Algorithm (the exact math)

  Porphyry takes two input points for each quadrant:
  - Lagnam (Ascendant) — ecliptic longitude where the eastern horizon crosses the ecliptic
  - MC (Madhya Kasha / Midheaven) — ecliptic longitude where the local meridian crosses the ecliptic

  These 2 points define 4 natural quadrants: Lagnam→MC, MC→Desc (opposite of Lagnam), Desc→IC (opposite of MC), IC→Lagnam.

  Each quadrant is trisected into 3 equal arcs of ecliptic longitude.

  Quadrant Lagnam→MC spans from ecliptic longitude L1 (Lagnam) to L2 (MC).
  Arc length = (L2 - L1) mod 360°.
  Divide into 3 equal segments of length (L2 - L1) / 3.

  House 1 cusp = L1 (Lagnam)
  House 2 cusp = L1 + (L2-L1)/3
  House 3 cusp = L1 + 2*(L2-L1)/3
  House 4 cusp = L2 (IC... wait, MC — let me redo this)

  Wait — let me be more careful. The classical Porphyry quadrants are:

  Quadrant 1: IC → Lagnam     (houses 1, 2, 3 cusps: Lagnam, Lagnam + (Lag-IC)/3, Lagnam - (Lag-IC)/3)
      Actually the standard formulation:

  Quadrant 1 (below horizon, rising):  4th cusp (IC) → 1st cusp (Lagnam)
    Houses 2, 3, 4 cusps derived by trisecting backward:
    cusp 4 = IC
    cusp 3 = IC + (Lagnam - IC) / 3
    cusp 2 = IC + 2 × (Lagnam - IC) / 3
    cusp 1 = Lagnam

  Quadrant 2 (above horizon, setting side): 1st cusp (Lagnam) → 10th cusp (MC)
    Wait, that's backwards. Let me redo.

  OK let me just give a clearer explanation without getting tangled in the direction conventions.

  The Porphyry cusp formula (cleaned up)

  Given:
  - Lagnam longitude (1st cusp)
  - MC longitude (10th cusp)
  - Desc = Lagnam + 180° (7th cusp)
  - IC = MC + 180° (4th cusp)

  The four quadrants are:
  - Quadrant I (houses 10→11→12→1): from MC to Lagnam
  - Quadrant II (houses 1→2→3→4): from Lagnam to IC
  - Quadrant III (houses 4→5→6→7): from IC to Desc
  - Quadrant IV (houses 7→8→9→10): from Desc to MC

  For each quadrant, the intermediate cusps are at 1/3 and 2/3 of the arc:

  arc = (end - start) mod 360°
  cusp_at_one_third = start + arc/3
  cusp_at_two_thirds = start + 2*arc/3

  So for Quadrant II (Lagnam → IC):
  - 1st cusp = Lagnam
  - 2nd cusp = Lagnam + (IC - Lagnam) mod 360° / 3
  - 3rd cusp = Lagnam + 2 × (IC - Lagnam) mod 360° / 3
  - 4th cusp = IC

  Same formula applied to all 4 quadrants gives all 12 cusps.

  Worked example — Chennai 19 April 2026, 05:55:28 IST

  Assume (illustrative Lahiri sidereal values):
  - Lagnam: 0°10' Meenam (Pisces) — sidereal longitude 330°10' = 330.167°
  - MC: 17°42' Dhanusu (Sagittarius) — sidereal longitude 257°42' = 257.700°
  - Desc: 0°10' Kanni (Virgo) = 150°10' = 150.167°
  - IC: 17°42' Mithunam (Gemini) = 77°42' = 77.700°

  Quadrant II (Lagnam → IC):
  - Arc from 330.167° to 77.700° (going forward through Meenam, Mesham, Rishabam, Mithunam) = (77.700 - 330.167) mod 360 = 107.533°
  - Cusp 2 = 330.167 + 107.533/3 = 330.167 + 35.844 = 366.011° = 6°01' Mesham (Aries)
  - Cusp 3 = 330.167 + 2×35.844 = 330.167 + 71.689 = 401.856° = 41.856° = 11°51' Rishabam (Taurus)
  - Cusp 4 = 77°42' Mithunam (IC, given)

  Quadrant III (IC → Desc):
  - Arc from 77.700° to 150.167° = 72.467°
  - Cusp 5 = 77.700 + 24.156 = 101.856° = 11°51' Karkatam (Cancer)
  - Cusp 6 = 77.700 + 48.311 = 126.011° = 6°01' Simmam (Leo)
  - Cusp 7 = 0°10' Kanni (Desc)

  And so on for Quadrants IV and I.

  Notice: house SIZES within a quadrant are equal (each house = 1/3 of its quadrant), but quadrants themselves are unequal (Q2 = 107.5°, Q3 = 72.5°), so houses in Q2
   are LARGER than houses in Q3.

  Key properties of Porphyry

  ┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Property      │                                                           Detail                                                           │
  ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Polar stability    │ ✅ Works at any latitude up to 90° — just divides ecliptic arcs mathematically. Unlike Placidus/Koch which fail above ~66° │
  ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ House size         │ Varies within chart — Q1 & Q4 equal to each other; Q2 & Q3 equal to each other; Q1≠Q2 generally                            │
  ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Intercepted signs  │ Possible but uncommon — occurs when a sign spans less than ~30° of a quadrant (rare)                                       │
  ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Simplicity         │ Pure ecliptic math; no need for Earth-rotation-based time calculations                                                     │
  ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Classical pedigree │ Oldest attested mathematical house system (Hellenistic 3rd c CE); foundational for later systems                           │
  ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Swiss Eph code     │ 'O' (character code)                                                                                                       │
  └────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Why Sripati derived FROM Porphyry

  Sripati's innovation: instead of treating cusps as house STARTS, treat them as house MIDDLES (bhava-madhya — "strongest point of the bhava"). So Sripati takes     
  Porphyry's cusps, then the MIDPOINTS between consecutive Porphyry cusps become the Sripati cusps. The result: Sripati bhava-madhya coincides with Porphyry cusp,   
  but Sripati's bhava extends half-house on each side.

  Where Porphyry is actually used

  1. Hellenistic natal astrology (Chris Brennan, modern traditional revival) — primary house system.
  2. Jyotish bhava alternate — some Parashari lineages use Porphyry instead of Sripati for bhava-chalit.
  3. Simplified Parashari computation — when software can't easily compute Sripati midpoints, Porphyry is a reasonable backup.
  4. Polar / extreme-latitude charts — when Placidus fails (above Arctic Circle), Porphyry works.

  Summary (one-liner)

  Porphyry = trisect each of the 4 ecliptic quadrants; each cusp marks the START of a house; polar-stable; simplest geometric house system in classical tradition;   
  parent of Parashari's Sripati.

  ---
  Was this what you wanted, or did you mean "in Porphyry, [specific detail]"?

❯ if lagna is 00-04-07 and MC is 08-13-50 calculate all houses as per sripathi method                                                                                                                                                                      
● Computing. First confirming my interpretation of your input:                                                                                                       
                                          
  Input:                                                                                                                                                               - Lagnam: 00-04-07 → sign 0 (Mesham), 4°, 07' → Mesham 4°07'00" = ecliptic longitude 4.1167°
  - MC: 08-13-50 → sign 8 (Dhanusu), 13°, 50' → Dhanusu 13°50'00" = ecliptic longitude 253.8333°                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  Derived:                                                                                                                                                             - Desc (7th cusp-point) = Lagnam + 180° = 184.1167° = Thulam 4°07'00"                                                                                                - IC (4th cusp-point) = MC + 180° = 73.8333° = Mithunam 13°50'00"                                                                                                  

  If my sign-indexing (0=Mesham) is wrong, redirect me.

  Sripati calculation — step by step

  Step 1: Compute the 4 quadrant arcs

  ┌─────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┬────────────┬────────────────────────────┐
  │    Quadrant     │                Span                 │ Arc length │ Trisection segment (arc/3) │
  ├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────┤

  ├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ Q2: Lagnam → IC │ 4.1167° → 73.8333°                  │ 69.7167°   │ 23.2389°                   │
  ├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ Q3: IC → Desc   │ 73.8333° → 184.1167°                │ 110.2833°  │ 36.7611°                   │
  ├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ Q4: Desc → MC   │ 184.1167° → 253.8333°               │ 69.7167°   │ 23.2389°                   │
  ├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │                 │ Total                               │ 360.00° ✓  │ —                          │
  └─────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┴────────────┴────────────────────────────┘

  Step 3: Bhava-sandhi (the junction points — midpoints between consecutive bhava-madhyas)

  Each sandhi = midpoint of two adjacent madhyas. This is where one bhava ends and the next begins.

  ├───────┼──────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────┼───────────┤
  │   8   │ Thulam 15°44'    │   Thulam 27°21'   │ Viruchigam 8°58' │  23°14'   │
  ├───────┼──────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────┼───────────┤
  │   9   │ Viruchigam 8°58' │ Viruchigam 20°36' │ Dhanusu 2°13'    │  23°14'   │
  ├───────┼──────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────┼───────────┤

  ├───────┼──────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────┼───────────┤

  └───────┴──────────────────┴───────────────────┴──────────────────┴───────────┘

  ---
  Observations on this chart

  1. Bhava 1, 4, 7, 10 are exactly 30° each — these are the Kendras (angles), and their madhyas are the Lagnam/IC/Desc/MC.
  2. Bhavas 2, 3, 8, 9 are the smallest (~23°14') — because Q2 and Q4 quadrants are shorter (69.72°) than Q1/Q3 (110.28°).
  3. Bhavas 5, 6, 11, 12 are the largest (~36°46') — they fall in the longer Q1/Q3 quadrants.
  4. Sandhi at Mithunam 2°13', Karkatam 2°13', Dhanusu 2°13', Magaram 2°13' — note the symmetric structure around the 4 Kendra quadrant junctions.
  5. Unequal bhavas are a feature, not a bug — this is why Sripati catches "planet in sign X but actually operating in bhava Y-1 or Y+1" that Whole-Sign misses. Your
   4°07' Mesham Lagnam is actually near the END of bhava 1 (which ends at Mesham 15°44'), so any planet in early Rishabam up to 15°44' Rishabam actually functions in
   bhava 2 (not bhava 3 as Whole Sign would say).

❯ how is Shadbalam calculated treating  Budhan benefic-malefic split                                                                                                                                                                       
● How Shadbalam is computed and where Budhan's benefic/malefic split matters                                                                                         

  Shadbalam (ஷட்பலம் = "six-fold strength") is the classical Parashari planetary strength system — BPHS Ch.27-29. Each graha gets 6 strength components summed, then   
  compared against minimum thresholds. A graha "passing" Shadbalam is strong enough to deliver its significations.
                                                                                                                                                                       ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt                                                                                                     The 6 components                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          ┌─────┬─────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   
  │  #  │    Component    │  Tamil   │                                                     What it measures                                                      │
  ├─────┼─────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │  1  │ Sthana Balam    │ ஸ்தான பலம்  │ Positional — exaltation, own-sign, divisional (Saptavargaja across 7 vargas), odd-even rasi, angular (Kendra), decanate   │
  │     │                 │          │ (Drekkana)                                                                                                                │   
  ├─────┼─────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │  2  │ Dik Balam       │ திக் பலம்   │ Directional — each graha has a preferred kendra (Guru/Budhan = East, Sooriyan/Sevvai = South, Sukkiran/Chandran = North,  │
  │     │                 │          │ Sani = West)                                                                                                              │   
  ├─────┼─────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │  3  │ Kala Balam      │ கால பலம்   │ Temporal — includes Paksha Balam which is where Budhan split matters                                                      │ 
  ├─────┼─────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │  4  │ Chesta Balam    │ சேஷ்டா பலம்  │ Motional — retrograde, combust, accelerating/decelerating                                                                │
  ├─────┼─────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │  5  │ Naisargika      │ நைசர்கிக    │ Natural ranking — fixed: Sooriyan > Chandran > Sukkiran > Guru > Budhan > Sevvai > Sani                                   │
  │     │ Balam           │ பலம்      │                                                                                                                           │  
  ├─────┼─────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │  6  │ Drik Balam      │ திருக் பலம்  │ Aspectual — sum of aspects received from benefics minus malefics                                                         │
  └─────┴─────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   

  Where Budhan's benefic/malefic split enters — Paksha Balam (inside Kala Balam)

  Classical rule (BPHS Ch.27 v.9-12):
  - Natural benefics (Sukkiran, Guru, Chandran-waxing) get STRONG in Shukla Paksham (waxing half of lunar month, tithi 1-15)
  - Natural malefics (Sooriyan, Sevvai, Sani, Rahu, Ketu, Chandran-waning) get STRONG in Krishna Paksham (waning half, tithi 16-30)

  For Budhan specifically — the classical rule is context-dependent (BPHS 27.11):

  ▎ Budhan is considered benefic when associated with natural benefics, malefic when associated with natural malefics, and neutral otherwise.

  Association = classical definition:
  1. Conjunction — Budhan in same rasi as another graha
  2. Aspect — Budhan receiving 7th aspect from another graha (or special aspects from Sevvai/Guru/Sani)
  3. Some schools add: same nakshatra sub-division (KP refinement)

  Paksha Balam formula (illustrating the Budhan split)

  For a graha classified as benefic (Budhan-benefic case):
  If in Shukla Paksham:  Paksha Balam = (Moon-Sun longitude difference in °) / 3 rupas
  If in Krishna Paksham: Paksha Balam = (180° - Moon-Sun diff) / 3 rupas

  For a graha classified as malefic (Budhan-malefic case):
  If in Shukla Paksham:  Paksha Balam = (180° - Moon-Sun diff) / 3 rupas
  If in Krishna Paksham: Paksha Balam = (Moon-Sun diff) / 3 rupas

  So Budhan's Paksha Balam can differ by up to ~60 rupas (20 each direction) depending on benefic/malefic classification — significant swing in total Shadbalam.     

  Chandran has a similar context-dependence (waxing vs waning)

  Chandran in Shukla Paksham (waxing, past Amavasai) is classified benefic.
  Chandran in Krishna Paksham (waning, past Pournami) is classified malefic.
  This split is unambiguous — just check paksham — no associate-lookup needed.

  Budhan's split is the one genuine ambiguity

  Worked example: 19 April 2026, Chennai, Budhan placed in Mesham with Sooriyan (conjunction). Under classical rule, Budhan associated with malefic Sooriyan → Budhan
   classified malefic for Paksha Balam. Today's tithi is Shukla Dwitiya (day 2) = Shukla Paksham, Moon-Sun longitude difference ~24°.

  - If Budhan treated as benefic: Paksha Balam = 24/3 = 8.0 rupas
  - If Budhan treated as malefic: Paksha Balam = (180-24)/3 = 52.0 rupas

  Delta of 44 rupas — huge swing in Shadbalam total. The classification decides whether Budhan "passes" or "fails" Shadbalam threshold.

  ---
  Q — Josi's Budhan classification policy for Shadbalam

  - A) Classical context-dependent evaluation — for each chart, Josi checks Budhan's associates (conjunction, aspect) and classifies per BPHS Ch.27 v.11 rule. Single
   output value for Shadbalam. Same for both user types.
  - B) Always-benefic default — simpler; some Parashari software defaults this way because Budhan is cognitively "neutral-benefic" in popular practice. Single       
  output. Same for both user types.
  - C) Compute BOTH values always (Budhan-as-benefic AND Budhan-as-malefic). Store both. B2C sees the classical context-dependent value (same as A). Astrologer sees 
  both values + the classical-rule classification + a "flip to other" toggle — per your earlier hint "so that astrologer will decide and AI will decide."
  - D) Compute BOTH + AI recommendation. Same as C but AI chat synthesizes: "classical rule says Budhan is malefic here due to Sooriyan conjunction, but if you      
  evaluate Budhan as benefic, Shadbalam shifts by 44 rupas — in this chart, that pushes Budhan's total strength above/below threshold." Surfaces the uncertainty to  
  both astrologer and AI-chat-reading end-user.
  - E) A for B2C + C for Astrologer — end-user sees single classical value (no cognitive load); astrologer sees both + classical rule + toggle. Your "AI will decide"
   hint maps to B2C applying classical rule automatically.
  - F) Other — tell me.

  Which?

✻ Baked for 1m 1s

❯ shadbala will be shown as  rupas or percentage or bothrupasand percentage                                                                                          
                                                                             
● Good question. Classical Shadbalam is expressed in rupas (ரூபம்). 1 rupa = 60 virupas (sub-unit). Each graha has a different minimum threshold, making raw rupas    
  non-intuitive at a glance.
                                                                                                                                                                       Classical Shadbalam thresholds per graha (BPHS Ch.27)
                                                                                                                                                                       ┌──────────┬───────────────┬─────────────────┐                                                                                                                       │  Graha   │ Minimum rupas │ Minimum virupas │                                                                                                                       ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤                                                                                                                       │ Sooriyan │      6.5      │       390       │                                                                                                                     
  ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Chandran │      6.0      │       360       │
  ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sevvai   │      5.0      │       300       │
  ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Budhan   │      7.0      │       420       │
  ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Guru     │      6.5      │       390       │
  ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sukkiran │      5.5      │       330       │
  ├──────────┼───────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sani     │      5.0      │       300       │
  └──────────┴───────────────┴─────────────────┘

  Percentage = actual_rupas / threshold × 100:
  - 100% = exactly at threshold (passes)
  ▎ 100% = strong
  - < 100% = weak (fails Shadbalam)

  Percentage normalizes across grahas — makes "Budhan at 7.2 rupas (103%)" vs "Sooriyan at 7.2 rupas (111%)" comparable at a glance.

  What modern Parashari software does

  ┌───────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     Software      │                     Display                     │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Jagannatha Hora   │ Both — rupas + percentage + component breakdown │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Parashara's Light │ Both — rupas + percentage                       │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Jyotishstar       │ Rupas + "pass/fail" color badge                 │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Drik Panchang     │ Percentage bar chart                            │
  ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Astrosage         │ Rupas only                                      │
  └───────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Q — Shadbalam display format

  - A) Rupas only — classical purity. Astrologer expected to know thresholds. B2C will struggle.
  - B) Percentage only — intuitive. Loses classical-purity reference. Astrologer may find it non-traditional.
  - C) Both for both user types — rupas + percentage + pass/fail badge. Same for B2C and Astrologer. ← my recommendation
  - D) Split by user type — B2C sees percentage + pass/fail badge (intuitive). Astrologer sees rupas + percentage + pass/fail + component breakdown (the 6 components
   individually: Sthana/Dik/Kala/Chesta/Naisargika/Drik in rupas per graha).
  - E) Both for both + component breakdown for both — full transparency for everyone. Dense but complete.
  - F) Other.

    Components: Sthana 2.10 · Dik 0.85 · Kala 2.30 · Chesta 0.95 · Naisargika 0.67 · Drik 0.65
    Threshold: 7.0 rupas minimum (BPHS Ch.27)

  Example for Option D B2C:
  Budhan Shadbalam: 107% (strong — passes classical threshold)

✻ Cogitated for 40s

❯ next give astakavargam scope ie sarvastagavargam after ekathipathya sothanai and trikona sothanai and how the all results will be summarised for end user and      
  astrologer

● Complete Ashtakavargam computation pipeline (BPHS Ch.66–67)

  Classical Parashari computes in 6 stages. Sarvashtakavargam appears at TWO points in the pipeline — raw (pre-shodhanai) and shodhit (post-shodhanai).

  Stage 1: Bhinnashtakavargam (BAV) — per-planet matrix

  For each of 7 grahas + Lagna: 8 contributors × 12 rasis = 8×12 matrix, values 0–8 bindus per cell.

  Stage 2: Raw Sarvashtakavargam (Raw SAV)

  Sum all 7 BAVs column-wise → one 12-rasi row, max 56 bindus per rasi.

  Stage 3: Trikona Shodhanai (Trinal Purification) — BPHS 66.1–5

  Per-BAV operation. For each trine group (1-5-9, 2-6-10, 3-7-11, 4-8-12), find the minimum bindu of the 3 trine-members and subtract from all 3. Applied to each    
  planet's BAV individually.

  Stage 4: Ekadhipatya Shodhanai (Dual-Lordship Purification) — BPHS 66.6–15

  Per-BAV operation. For each graha ruling two rasis (Sevvai=Mesham+Viruchigam, Budhan=Mithunam+Kanni, Guru=Dhanusu+Meenam, Sukkiran=Rishabam+Thulam,
  Sani=Magaram+Kumbham), apply the complex dual-ownership reduction rules. Applied after Trikona Shodhanai.

  Stage 5: Shodhit Sarvashtakavargam (Shodhit SAV) — the "real" SAV

  Sum the doubly-shodhit BAVs column-wise → this is the Sarvashtakavargam to interpret. Raw SAV from Stage 2 is classically NOT used for interpretation (B.V. Raman: 
  "never judge a house by raw bindu counts").

  Stage 6: Sodhya Pinda — BPHS Ch.67

  For each graha: Sodhya Pinda = shodhit BAV × Graha Pinda × Rashi Pinda. Feeds Ayurdaya (longevity) and Bhuktiphala (period results).

  Optional Stage 7: Kaksha Vibhaga — each rasi subdivided into 8 kakshas (3°45' each), ruled by grahas in sequence, for finer strength.

  ---
  Summary format — B2C end user

  The 4 matrices (Raw BAV · Trikona-Shodhit BAV · Ekadhipatya-Shodhit BAV · Sodhya Pinda) are cognitively overwhelming for a non-astrologer. Proposed B2C summary    
  approach:

  Surface only the post-shodhanai Sarvashtakavargam, translated to bhava strength using BPHS Ch.6 purpose mapping (already locked in DECISIONS 1.8):

  Your bhava strength (from Sarvashtakavargam, post-shodhanai):

  Strong (≥ 30 bindus):
    Bhava 10 (Career): 34 bindus — strong ✓
    Bhava 5 (Intellect, progeny): 32 bindus — strong ✓
    Bhava 1 (Self): 31 bindus — strong ✓

  Moderate (25-29 bindus):
    Bhava 11 (Gains): 28 bindus
    Bhava 7 (Partner): 27 bindus
    Bhava 9 (Fortune, dharma): 26 bindus

  Needs effort (< 25 bindus):
    Bhava 12 (Losses, retreats): 22 bindus
    Bhava 6 (Health, enemies): 20 bindus
    Bhava 8 (Transformation): 18 bindus

  - No raw BAV matrices by default.
  - No Sodhya Pinda exposure (too technical).
  - Expand-on-demand toggle → shows the per-graha contribution if requested.
  - Colored visualization (heatmap).

  Summary format — Astrologer

  Full classical transparency. Proposed astrologer view has 5 tabs + 1 panel:

  Tab 1: Raw BAV matrices — 7 planets × 12 rasis × 8 contributors, with contributor-trace per cell (which 8 contributors gave bindus for this cell).

  Tab 2: Trikona-Shodhit BAV — per-planet post-Trikona matrices. Shows which trine-group minimum was subtracted per graha.

  Tab 3: Ekadhipatya-Shodhit BAV — per-planet post-Ekadhipatya (doubly-shodhit) matrices. Shows the dual-lordship reduction trace per ruling graha.

  Tab 4: Sarvashtakavargam (SAV) — both Raw SAV + Shodhit SAV side-by-side, column totals per rasi, with bhava mapping per BPHS Ch.6.

  Tab 5: Sodhya Pinda — per-graha Pinda values with Graha Pinda + Rashi Pinda factors exposed. Feeds into Ayurdaya module (E5b).

  Panel: Kaksha Vibhaga toggle — optional finer subdivision, per-graha kaksha strength across 96 kakshas (12 rasis × 8 kakshas each).

  Plus:
  - Transit Ashtakavargam — current transit strength per graha per rasi (for muhurta + transit phalita).
  - Divisional Ashtakavargam toggle — compute SAV/BAV for D9, D10 (see scope Q below).
  - Contributor-trace drill-down per cell.

  Two questions for you to lock

  Q1 — B2C summary approach:

  - A) Proposed above — bhava-strength summary (post-shodhanai only), 3-tier (strong/moderate/needs effort) with BPHS Ch.6 purpose mapping. Raw BAV hidden by        
  default; expand-on-demand.

  - D) Other.

  Q2 — Divisional Ashtakavargam scope:

  Classically, BAV/SAV computes on rasi chart (D1) only. Modern practice varies.

  - X) D1 (rasi) only — strict classical (BPHS). Astrologer can't compute D9/D10 Ashtakavargam.
  - Y) D1 + D9 (Navamsa) — modern Parashari extension (some schools).
  - Z) D1 + D9 + D10 (Dasamsa) — broader modern.
  - W) All 16 vargas — no classical support, high compute cost, astrologer-only opt-in toggle.

  Which for each (answer e.g., "A + X" or "A + Y")?

   Dhanusu        4       3                 3                 Guru rules

   Meenam         6       5                 5                 Guru rules
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   Total          48      34                34
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Trikona Shodhanai trace:
    Trine (1,5,9)  = Mesham(5), Simmam(5), Dhanusu(4)     → min=4, subtract
    Trine (2,6,10) = Rishabam(3), Kanni(3), Magaram(5)    → min=3, subtract
    Trine (3,7,11) = Mithunam(2), Thulam(2), Kumbham(5)   → min=2, subtract
    Trine (4,8,12) = Karkatam(4), Viruchigam(4), Meenam(6)→ min=4, subtract...
    Wait: per BPHS 66.1-5, trine shodhanai doesn't simply subtract the min uniformly.
    Full classical rule: in each trine, if all 3 rasis have non-zero bindus, reduce to
    one "strongest" rasi's bindu; others become zero (Parashara method). If one is
    zero already, leave others unchanged. Variants exist.

    [Trace shown is Josi's chosen variant — Phaladeepika version:
     "subtract the minimum of the trine from all three trine members"]

  haven't locked which Shodhanai variant. Flag: which Trikona Shodhanai method?)

  Graha Pinda and Rashi Pinda are fixed classical tables (BPHS 67.1-14). Sodhya Pinda per graha feeds directly into Ayurdaya (longevity) calculation in E5b — which, 
  per DECISIONS 1.7 style, is astrologer-gated for ethical reasons.

  ---
  Kaksha Vibhaga — what it is

  Concept: Each of 12 rasis is subdivided into 8 kakshas of 3°45' each. So total = 12 × 8 = 96 kakshas across the zodiac. Each kaksha is "ruled" by one of 7 grahas  
  (Sooriyan, Chandran, Sevvai, Budhan, Guru, Sukkiran, Sani) in a classical repeating sequence.

  Why: Every rasi gives a graha 0-8 bindus in its BAV (8 contributors). Kaksha Vibhaga asks: WHICH of the 8 contributors gave the bindu in this specific 3°45' window
   where the planet actually sits. So instead of "Sooriyan gets 5 bindus in Magaram" (a 30° span), Kaksha tells you "Sooriyan in the Guru-ruled kaksha of Magaram    
  gets bindu from Guru specifically."

  Use case: Transit phalita (gochara). When a graha transits a rasi, its effect is modulated by which kaksha it's in. Classical astrologers use Kaksha to time       
  within-rasi transit events (a planet entering a favorable kaksha triggers benefic events even mid-rasi).

  Kaksha sequence in each rasi (classical): Sani → Guru → Sevvai → Sooriyan → Sukkiran → Budhan → Chandran → [repeat Sani from start if needed]. So each rasi's 8    
  kakshas follow this order, each 3°45' wide.

  Sample Kaksha Vibhaga for Sooriyan in Magaram

  ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  KAKSHA VIBHAGA — Sooriyan's BAV contribution by Kaksha within Magaram
  ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Kaksha #  Longitude range      Kaksha     Sooriyan got   Active if Sun
             within Magaram       lord       bindu from?    transits here?
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   1         0°00' - 3°45'        Sani       Yes            Strong
   2         3°45' - 7°30'        Guru       Yes            Strong
   3         7°30' - 11°15'       Sevvai     Yes            Strong
   4         11°15' - 15°00'      Sooriyan   No             Weak
   5         15°00' - 18°45'      Sukkiran   No             Weak
   6         18°45' - 22°30'      Budhan     Yes            Strong
   7         22°30' - 26°15'      Chandran   No             Weak
   8         26°15' - 30°00'      Sani       Yes            Strong
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
                                  Magaram BAV total: 5 of 8 contributors
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Practical read: When Sooriyan transits the 3rd kaksha of Magaram (7°30'-11°15' Magaram), Sooriyan's transit is strong (he got a bindu there). When he's in kaksha 4
   (11°15'-15°00' Magaram), weak. This is the classical "within-rasi" precision Kaksha provides.

  For B2C: Kaksha is too granular — hide entirely.
  For Astrologer: optional toggle, shows per-graha kaksha contribution across all 96 kakshas.

● Logged. Now the pending sub-flag from the sample rendering:

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q — Trikona Shodhanai variant
                                                                                                                                                                     
  Classical Trikona Shodhanai has two competing interpretations in Parashari literature:

  Variant 1 — BPHS strict (zero-out rule) per BPHS Ch.66 v.1-5:
  For each trine (1-5-9, 2-6-10, 3-7-11, 4-8-12):
  - If all three trine rasis have non-zero bindus → all three become zero (aggressive purification — "common strength is shared, hence not distinctive")
  - If any of the three has zero already → leave all three unchanged
  - If only two non-zero → subtract the smaller non-zero from the larger, zero-out the smaller, leave the zero-value as zero

  Example (5, 3, 4): all non-zero → all become 0.

  Variant 2 — Phaladeepika subtract-minimum:
  For each trine:
  - Find the minimum bindu in the trine
  - Subtract that minimum from all three

  Example (5, 3, 4): min=3 → becomes (2, 0, 1).

  Impact: Variant 1 is more aggressive (zeroes more cells). Variant 2 is more conservative (preserves positional differences). Totals across all 12 rasis after      
  Trikona Shodhanai:

  - Variant 1 (zero-out): total bindus reduce dramatically (~40-60% reduction)
  - Variant 2 (subtract-min): total bindus reduce moderately (~25-35% reduction)

  Modern software:
  - Jagannatha Hora — offers both, defaults to Variant 2 (subtract-minimum)
  - Parashara's Light — Variant 2
  - Some Tamil traditional panchang software — Variant 1

  Classical citation differences:
  - BPHS Ch.66 v.1-2 Sanskrit text has been interpreted both ways by commentators. The translation ambiguity is real.
  - B.V. Raman's A Catechism of Astrology uses Variant 2.
  - P.V.R. Narasimha Rao (JH) uses Variant 2.

  Q — Trikona Shodhanai variant:

  - A) Variant 2 (Phaladeepika subtract-minimum) — matches JH, Parashara's Light, B.V. Raman. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation (majority modern
  practice).
  - B) Variant 1 (BPHS strict zero-out) — stricter classical reading. Same for both.
  - C) Variant 2 default + astrologer toggle — let astrologer switch to Variant 1 per profile for comparison/research.
  - D) Both computed always — as sibling rules (F8 aggregation); B2C sees Variant 2 value; Astrologer sees both.
  - E) Research — spawn agent to verify actual Sanskrit interpretation + which variant each classical commentator favored.

  Which?

✻ Baked for 1m 12s

❯ what is BPHS

● BPHS — Brihat Parasara Hora Sastram (பிருஹத் பராசர ஓரா சாஸ்திரம்)

  Full name: Bṛhat Parāśara Horā Śāstra (Sanskrit: बृहत्पराशरहोराशास्त्र)
  Meaning: "The Great Book of Parashara's Predictive Science" (Brihat = great/comprehensive, Parashara = the author-sage, Hora = predictive astrology, Shastra =     
  canonical treatise)

  What it is

  The foundational canonical text of Parashari Vedic astrology. It is to Vedic astrology what Euclid's Elements is to geometry — the single-most-cited classical     
  source for every technique in the Parashari tradition.

  Attribution and dating

  - Attributed to Sage Parashara (Parāśara / பராசரர்) — ancient Vedic sage, father of Vyasa (who compiled the Mahabharata). Traditional dating ~3000 BCE.
  - Scholarly dating: composition dated to ~4th–7th century CE with accretions through ~11th century. The text we have today is a compilation by multiple hands      
  carrying forward Parashara's lineage teachings over centuries.
  - Form: Dialogue between Sage Parashara and his disciple Maitreya, ~97 chapters, ~5000 Sanskrit verses (slokas).

  Why Josi references it constantly

  BPHS is the primary source for virtually every Parashari technique Josi implements. Specific chapters driving Josi's calculations:

  ┌──────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┐
  │ BPHS Chapter │                         Topic                         │        Josi PRD         │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │     Ch.6     │ Shodashavarga (16 divisional charts) + their purposes │ Locked in DECISIONS 1.9 │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │   Ch.27–29   │ Shadbala (six-fold strength)                          │ DECISIONS 1.7           │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.36     │ Raja Yogas (~60 core yogas)                           │ E4a                     │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.40     │ Sade Sati (Saturn's 7.5-year transit)                 │ E6a                     │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.47     │ Ashtottari Dasai (108-year dasa)                      │ E1a, DECISIONS 1.6      │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.48     │ Yogini Dasai (36-year dasa)                           │ E1a                     │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.50     │ Chara Dasai                                           │ E1b                     │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.53     │ Kalachakra Dasai                                      │ E1b                     │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.66     │ Trikona Shodhanai + Ekadhipatya Shodhanai             │ E2a, DECISIONS 1.8      │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │    Ch.67     │ Sodhya Pinda                                          │ E2a, feeds E5b          │
  ├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
  │   Ch.72–74   │ Ayurdaya / Pindayu (longevity — 3 methods)            │ E5b                     │
  └──────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

  BPHS vs other classical sources

  BPHS is the Parashari-school canon. Parallel canonical texts exist for other traditions:

  ┌─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┐
  │            Tradition            │         Primary canon         │    Equivalent role    │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Parashari (Parasara school)     │ BPHS — this one               │ Josi's primary source │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Jaimini                         │ Jaimini Upadesha Sutras (JUS) │ E3 Jaimini System     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Tajaka (annual-chart tradition) │ Tajaka Neelakanthi            │ E5 Varshaphala        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ KP (Krishnamurti Paddhati)      │ KP Reader Vols 1–6            │ E9 KP System          │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Hellenistic Western             │ Dorotheus, Valens, Ptolemy    │ E8 Western Depth      │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Kerala Prasna                   │ Prasna Marga                  │ E10 Prasna            │
  └─────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘

  BPHS is the "constitution" of the Parashari tradition; other traditions have their own constitutions. Josi's DEFAULT tradition is Parashari, so BPHS is cited most 
  often.

  Modern English translations commonly used

  - R. Santhanam translation (Ranjan Publications, 1984) — 3-volume set; most cited in academic/modern Parashari practice.
  - G.C. Sharma translation (Sagar Publications) — widely used in North India.
  - Girish Chand Sharma translation — another widely cited version.
  - P.V.R. Narasimha Rao (founder of Jagannatha Hora software) — maintains annotated BPHS references in his works.

  In Tamil: Brihat Parasara Hora Sastram published by Tamil jyotish publishers like Poompukar Pathippakam.

  ├───────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Nakshatra count           │ 27 for everything (including dasai starting-lord mapping)        │ 3.7                 │
  ├───────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Varga purpose metadata    │ BPHS Ch.6 canonical per-varga purpose string                     │ 1.9                 │
  ├───────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Language display          │ Tamil phonetic + Sanskrit IAST; 14 ambiguities locked            │ 1.5                 │
  └───────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘

  Remaining E1a-specific questions (7 still pending):
