  Q6 — Sign-ingress event scope (which grahas emit ingress events?)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  Background. Ingresses are sign-boundary crossings — when a graha moves from one rasi to the next. Graha frequencies vary drastically.                                                                                                                                                                                                     ┌───────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                       
  │         Graha         │        Ingress frequency        │ Events per 30yr │                    Classical significance                    │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Chandran              │ ~2.25 days                      │      ~5000      │ Too frequent; not typically tracked as "events"              │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sooriyan (Sankranti)  │ ~30 days                        │      ~360       │ HUGE in Hindu calendar — each Sankranti named and celebrated │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sevvai                │ ~45 days                        │      ~240       │ Moderate; notable in Tamil practice                          │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Budhan                │ ~20-60 days (retrograde varies) │      ~180       │ Moderate                                                     │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sukkiran              │ ~20-60 days (retrograde varies) │      ~180       │ Moderate                                                     │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Guru (guru peyarchi)  │ ~12 months                      │       ~30       │ Major — widely tracked                                       │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sani (shani peyarchi) │ ~29.5 months                    │       ~12       │ Major — widely tracked                                       │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Rahu / Ketu           │ ~18 months (reverse)            │       ~20       │ Major — widely tracked                                       │
  └───────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Scope options

  Option A — Outer only (Guru, Sani, Rahu, Ketu)

  PRD current. ~92 events per 30 years. Matches what Tamil Vakya panchangams call out as "peyarchi" (sign changes). Sooriyan Sankrantis delegated to festival engine 
  (per DECISIONS 3.12 already covering Sankranti as tithi-recurring event).

  Option B — Outer + Sevvai (5 grahas)

  Add Sevvai ingresses (~8 per year) = ~332 events per 30yr. Tamil tradition gives Sevvai peyarchi moderate attention (Sevvai Sani-Raghu conjunction, Sevvai in 8th, 
  etc.).

  Option C — All 7 grahas (exclude Chandran as too frequent)

  Sooriyan Sankranti + Sevvai + Budhan + Sukkiran + outer = ~1000 events per 30yr. Comprehensive; high event density.

  Option D — Outer + Sooriyan Sankrantis (E6a scope includes Sun)

  Hybrid — outer planets for E6a transit engine + Sooriyan's 12 Sankrantis per year as named-event overlay. ~452 events per 30yr. Deduplication concern: Sooriyan    
  Sankrantis already in festival engine (DECISIONS 3.12 festival calendar). If both engines emit Sankrantis, need reconciliation.

  Option E — Configurable per user

  Astrologer chooses which grahas to track; B2C gets fixed default (Outer only).

  Deduplication note with DECISIONS 3.12

  Festival calendar (locked) already emits Sankranti events (Sooriyan into new rasi). E6a could:
  - Reference festival engine's Sankranti events (single source of truth)
  - Emit its own Sankranti events (duplication)
  - Skip Sankrantis entirely (covered elsewhere)

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Configurable; default = outer (Jupiter, Saturn, Rahu/Ketu)
  - Parashara's Light: Outer + Sevvai + Sooriyan Sankrantis
  - Drik Panchang: Outer + all Sankrantis (Sooriyan)
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Focus on Guru peyarchi, Sani peyarchi, Rahu-Ketu peyarchi
  - Astrosage transit section: All 7 grahas (excludes Chandran)

  Q6 — Sign-ingress event scope for E6a:

  - A) Outer only (Guru, Sani, Rahu, Ketu) — PRD current. ~92 events/30yr. Sooriyan Sankrantis delegated to festival engine. Matches Tamil Vakya peyarchi focus.     
  - B) Outer + Sevvai — add Sevvai peyarchi. ~332 events/30yr.
  - C) All 7 grahas (exclude Chandran) — comprehensive. ~1000 events/30yr.
  - D) Outer + Sooriyan Sankrantis — cross-reference festival engine for Sankrantis (no duplication); transit engine owns outer-planet ingresses.
  - E) B2C: outer only; Astrologer: all 7 grahas — split by user type; calculation stores all 7; UI renders per user type.
  - F) Astrologer-configurable per profile (check-all grahas); B2C fixed outer-only.
  - G) Other.

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q7 — Matching bar vs JH 7.x for transit boundary dates (final E6a question)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Background. Like E1a Q9 (dasai boundaries) and E2a Q5 (Ashtakavargam ±0), E6a golden-chart tests need a precision bar against JH 7.x reference values. But transit   events are heterogeneous:                                                                                                                                          

  Event type precision characteristics

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┐   
  │                           Event type                           │                         Precision ceiling                          │   Natural tolerance    │   
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┤   
  │ Sign ingress (Guru/Sani/Sevvai/Rahu/Ketu sign boundary         │ Pure arithmetic on sidereal longitude mod 30° — sub-second         │ ±1 second achievable   │   
  │ crossing)                                                      │ achievable                                                         │                        │   
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┤   
  │ Sade Sati phase boundary                                       │ Same as sign ingress for Sani                                      │ ±1 second achievable   │   
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┤   
  │ Eclipse contact (P1/U1/Max/U4/P4)                              │ Astronomical event from Swiss Eph ephemeris data                   │ ±1-5 seconds           │   
  │                                                                │                                                                    │ achievable             │   
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┤   
  │ Retrograde direct/retrograde flip                              │ Pure arithmetic on speed sign                                      │ ±1 minute achievable   │   
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┘   

  Options

  Option A — ±1 day for all event types

  Same as dasai matching bar (E1a Q9). Pragmatic for B2C display. Matches Tamil Vakya panchangam convention ("Guru peyarchi on March 15"). Excessively loose for     
  astronomical events that are inherently precise.

  Option B — ±1 hour for ingresses + Sade Sati + retrograde flips; ±1 second for eclipse contacts

  Tighter for events naturally achievable at higher precision. Eclipse contacts should match Swiss Eph / NASA computations to seconds. Ingresses at hourly resolution
   catches timezone/DST bugs.

  Option C — ±1 second for all event types

  Academic-grade across the board. Requires identical ayanamsa + identical node type (Mean vs True) + identical ephemeris version. Achievable if both Josi and JH use
   matching SIDM_LAHIRI + swe.MEAN_NODE + same Swiss Eph binary.

  Option D — Event-type-specific bars

  - Sign ingress: ±1 hour
  - Sade Sati phase: ±1 day (classical interpretation doesn't depend on sub-hour precision)
  - Eclipse contact (P1/U1/Max/U4/P4): ±1 second
  - Retrograde flip: ±1 minute

  Tailored to actual classical/astronomical use cases. More nuanced test assertions.

  Classical + modern convention

  - Tamil Vakya panchangams print Guru peyarchi to MINUTE precision ("Guru peyarchi at 14:32 IST on March 15, 2028")
  - Jagannatha Hora shows ingresses to minute precision; eclipses to second precision
  - NASA eclipse catalog uses second-precision for contact times
  - Drik Panchang publishes ingresses to minute precision

  Practical reality

  Transit events are astronomical, not classical-rule-derived. Given locked ayanamsa (Lahiri, DECISIONS 1.2) and locked node type (Mean + True both per DECISIONS    
  1.1), matching JH at ±1 minute for ingresses / ±1 second for eclipses is achievable if Josi uses the same node type as JH for the comparison.

  Q7 — Matching bar vs JH 7.x for transit boundary dates:

  - A) ±1 day for all event types — same convention as dasai boundaries. Pragmatic; loose. Same for both user types.
  - B) ±1 hour ingresses + ±1 second eclipses — tighter for astronomical events; still practical for CI testing.
  - C) ±1 second for all event types — academic-grade; highest test discipline.
  - D) Event-type-specific bars (Option D above: ingress ±1 hour, Sade Sati ±1 day, eclipse ±1 second, retrograde flip ±1 minute) — tailored per event's natural     
  precision and classical usage. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Worked for 35s

Step B — Calculation pipeline (E1b inserts after E1a)

  2. E1a dasais computed (Vimshottari, Yogini, Ashtottari)
  3. E1b Chara Dasai engine:
    - Input: Atmakaraka placement, Lagna sign
    - Identify direction (forward/backward from Lagna sign)

    - Emit temporal_hierarchy (MD → AD → PD for Chara)

    - Emit temporal_hierarchy (MD → AD → PD for Narayana)

  Dhanusu=10, Magaram=4, Kumbham=4, Meenam=10 — classical BPHS Ch.53)
    - Emit temporal_hierarchy
  6. All 3 emit to chart_reading_view.current_dasas for unified query

  - Q6 — Kalachakra rasi-year table source (BPHS Ch.53 vs Jataka Parijata)
  - Q7 — Cross-source aggregation — ship BPHS default + Rangacharya variant as sibling rules, or curate single canonical?

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Atmakaraka determination: 7 grahas or 8?

  Background. Atmakaraka (AK) = "soul-significator" — the graha with the highest longitude-within-sign (0°-30°). In Jaimini tradition, AK is the most important chart
   element; it anchors Chara Dasai (starting sign and direction), Narayana Dasai (Karakamsa derivation), and Karakamsa-based yoga evaluation.

  Two classical variants

  Variant A — 7-graha Atmakaraka (Parashari-influenced Jaimini)

  Consider only Sooriyan, Chandran, Sevvai, Budhan, Guru, Sukkiran, Sani. Pick the graha with highest longitude-within-sign. Rahu excluded.

  Variant B — 8-graha Atmakaraka (traditional Jaimini)

  Add Rahu. But Rahu's longitude-within-sign treats it as (30° − actual_long_within_sign) because Rahu moves retrograde — "higher remaining" is equivalent to the    
  other grahas' "higher elapsed."

  Example: If Rahu is at 5° within a rasi, its "AK candidacy value" is 30−5 = 25°. If Guru is at 20° within its rasi, Guru's value is 20°. In this case Rahu (25°)   
  has higher AK score than Guru (20°).

  Classical sources

  - Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.1-5 — 8 karakas including Rahu (traditional Jaimini position)
  - BPHS Ch.40 (Jaimini chapter) — ambiguous; both variants attributed
  - Irangati Rangacharya commentary — 8-karaka with Rahu convention
  - Sanjay Rath (Puri Jaimini lineage) — 8-karaka strict
  - B.V. Raman — 7-karaka (excludes Rahu as "non-planetary")
  - K.N. Rao — 8-karaka
  - P.V.R. Narasimha Rao — 8-karaka default, 7-karaka as option

  Practical impact

  With 7 karakas: AK is always one of the 7 visible grahas. With 8, Rahu frequently takes AK (~12% of charts where Rahu's "remaining-longitude" exceeds all 7 grahas'
   elapsed-longitudes).

  When Rahu is AK (variant B only), interpretation is significantly different — Rahu-AK charts are classically flagged as "karmic / foreign / unconventional
  soul-path."

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: 8-karaka default (Rahu included with 30°− rule); 7-karaka as optional toggle
  - Parashara's Light: 8-karaka default
  - Astrosage: 8-karaka default
  - Raman's published charts: 7-karaka

  Q1 — Atmakaraka determination:

  - A) 8-karaka (include Rahu) default — traditional Jaimini per Upadesha Sutras + Rangacharya + Rath + Rao + JH/PL. Rahu's AK candidacy uses 30° −
  longitude_within_sign. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation (dominant modern Jaimini practice).
  - B) 7-karaka (exclude Rahu) default — matches B.V. Raman's published treatment; Parashari-influenced Jaimini.
  - C) 8-karaka default + astrologer toggle to 7 — JH convention. B2C always 8.
  - D) Research — verify whether Tamil Parashari-Jaimini practitioners (Rath lineage influence in TN) uniformly use 8 or 7.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Crunched for 1m 5s

❯ option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E1b Q1 locked: Option A — 8-karaka Atmakaraka. Include Rahu; Rahu's AK candidacy = 30° − longitude_within_sign. Traditional Jaimini per Upadesha Sutras +          
  Rangacharya + Rath + Rao + JH/PL. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Chara Dasai direction rule                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  Background. Chara Dasai is sign-based. Starting rasi = rasi containing Atmakaraka (Karakamsa rasi). Direction through the zodiac (forward or backward) depends on    classical rule. Two major interpretations.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Variant A — BPHS Ch.50 rule (parity-based)

  - If starting rasi is odd (Mesham, Mithunam, Simmam, Thulam, Dhanusu, Kumbham) → direction forward (1 → 2 → 3 ...)
  - If starting rasi is even (Rishabam, Kadagam, Kanni, Viruchigam, Magaram, Meenam) → direction backward (12 → 11 → 10 ...)

  Simple rule. Matches many modern Parashari implementations.

  Variant B — Rangacharya commentary (lord-placement-based)

  Direction determined by where the lord of the starting rasi sits:
  - If starting rasi's lord is in a position counted forward from the rasi → forward direction
  - If backward → backward direction

  More nuanced. Requires lord-placement analysis for each chart. Used by Sanjay Rath (Puri lineage).

  Variant C — Irangati Rangacharya modern synthesis

  Hybrid — starts with Variant A parity rule, but Rangacharya adds exceptions for specific placements (e.g., Atmakaraka in a kendra from Karakamsa reverses
  direction). Jaimini scholar programme materials use this.

  Variant D — Iyer/Iyengar Tamil Parashari variant

  Some Tamil Parashari lineages use Lagna-sign-parity (not starting rasi parity) to determine direction. Minority school.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (BPHS parity) default; Variant B (Rangacharya) as toggle
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A default
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC teachings: Variant B/C (Rangacharya lineage)
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams (where Chara Dasai published): Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A

  Classical source weighting

  - BPHS Ch.50 verses are unambiguous on Variant A
  - Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.8 is terse and can be read either way
  - Rangacharya's Kalpalatha commentary explicitly endorses Variant B

  Practical impact

  ~50% of charts have different dasai timeline orders between A and B (since starting rasi parity and lord-placement direction disagree in ~half of cases). Major    
  interpretive divergence — choosing the wrong variant means completely different life periods.

  Q2 — Chara Dasai direction rule:

  - A) BPHS Ch.50 parity rule (Variant A) — odd rasi forward, even rasi backward. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Tamil Vakya default. Same for both user types. ← my  
  recommendation (classical BPHS primary source + dominant software convention).
  - B) Rangacharya lord-placement rule (Variant B) — Sanjay Rath Puri lineage; classical commentary-driven.
  - C) Variant A default + astrologer toggle to B — matches JH convention.
  - D) Hybrid Rangacharya modern (Variant C) — parity rule + exceptions.
  - E) Research — verify which Tamil lineage dominates before locking.
  - F) Other.

    Which?

❯ option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E1b Q2 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.50 parity rule. Odd starting rasi → forward direction; Even starting rasi → backward direction. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage +    
  Tamil Vakya. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q3 — Chara Dasai period-length formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Background. Each rasi in the Chara Dasai sequence has a specific period length in years. Formula: "count from the rasi to its lord's placement" — but the exact      rule varies classically.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  Variant A — BPHS Ch.50 count-to-lord (standard)

  For each rasi R with lord L:
    count = positions from R to rasi containing L (counting both inclusive; forward if direction = forward, backward if direction = backward)

    If count == 1 (lord is in R itself):
      period = 12 years
    Else:
      period = count - 1 years

  Example: If Mesham is the starting rasi (Sevvai is lord), and Sevvai sits in Kadagam:
  - Forward count Mesham → Kadagam = 4
  - Period = 4 − 1 = 3 years

  Edge: if Sevvai sits in Mesham itself (count=1), period = 12 years.

  Direction of counting matches Chara Dasai direction (forward for odd-start, backward for even-start per Q2).

  Variant B — Jaimini Sutras "count to lord" (subtle difference)

  Same count logic but uses forward-count regardless of dasai direction. Simpler but classically less defensible for backward-running dasais.

  Variant C — Rangacharya commentary exceptions

  Modifies Variant A with exceptions:
  - If lord is exalted in its placement: add 1 year
  - If lord is debilitated: subtract 1 year
  - If lord is in its own/moolatrikona sign: add 1 year
  - Minimum period = 1 year, maximum = 12 years

  More nuanced; used by Sanjay Rath Puri lineage.

  Variant D — Rahu/Ketu co-rulership handling

  For Viruchigam (lord = Sevvai AND Ketu per some schools) and Kumbham (lord = Sani AND Rahu per some schools), use Ketu/Rahu instead of Sevvai/Sani in period       
  computation. Classical but controversial.

  Some schools use BOTH co-rulers — take longer/shorter period. Others use traditional rulers only (Sevvai + Sani).

  Total cycle length

  - Variant A: ~144 years (sum of all 12 rasi periods)
  - Variant B: same ~144 years
  - Variant C: 144 ± exceptions (~130-160 years depending on chart)
  - Variant D: varies with co-ruler choice

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A default; Variant C as advanced toggle
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A default
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant C (Rangacharya)
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Variant A
  - K.N. Rao Chara Dasa: Variant A with Variant D (uses Ketu for Viruchigam, Rahu for Kumbham)

  Q3 — Chara Dasai period-length formula:

  - A) BPHS Ch.50 count-to-lord (Variant A), traditional rulers only (Sevvai for Viruchigam, Sani for Kumbham). Matches JH default + PL + Tamil Vakya. Same for both 
  user types.
  - B) Variant A + Rahu/Ketu co-rulership (Variant D combined) — Ketu for Viruchigam, Rahu for Kumbham. Matches K.N. Rao's published treatment.
  - C) Variant A + astrologer toggle to Rangacharya (Variant C) — exceptions for exalted/debilitated/own-sign lords. JH convention.
  - D) Full Variant C (Rangacharya) default — Puri Jaimini lineage.
  - E) Research — verify K.N. Rao vs Rath vs JH consensus before locking.
  - F) Other.

  Which? 
                                                                                                          
option A

● E1b Q3 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.50 count-to-lord formula, traditional rulers only (Sevvai for Viruchigam, Sani for Kumbham). Period = count−1 years (forward or  
  backward per Q2 direction); 12 years if lord in rasi itself. Total cycle ~144 years. Matches JH + PL + Tamil Vakya. Same for both user types.

  ---
  Q4 — Narayana Dasai starting rule

  Background. Narayana Dasai is a SECOND Jaimini sign-based dasai distinct from Chara. It produces a different MD sequence than Chara with different period lengths. 
  Used for event manifestation timing (macro life-event milestones). Starting rasi determined by a classical rule — and here's where Narayana has two major schools. 

  Variant A — Start from 7th-from-Lagna rasi (BPHS Ch.50 "Narayana" section)

  The 7th rasi counted from Lagna is the starting Mahadasa. Direction determined by parity (same as Chara Q2).

  Example: Lagna = Mesham. 7th from Mesham = Thulam. Narayana Dasai starts with Thulam Mahadasai.

  Classical rationale: 7th house represents "otherness / partner / foreign elements" — Narayana Dasai anchors on this axis for event-timing.

  Variant B — Start from Karakamsa rasi (Atmakaraka's Navamsa position)

  The rasi that Atmakaraka occupies in the Navamsa (D9) chart is the Karakamsa rasi. Narayana Dasai starts from Karakamsa.

  Example: If Guru is Atmakaraka and Guru in D9 is in Simmam → Karakamsa = Simmam → Narayana starts with Simmam Mahadasai.

  Classical rationale: Karakamsa represents "soul-level expression" — Narayana Dasai runs on the karmic timeline anchored there. Associated with Jaimini Upadesha    
  Sutras' treatment.

  Variant C — Start from Lagna itself (simplest)

  Narayana Dasai starts with Lagna rasi, same as Chara. But this makes Chara and Narayana produce identical timelines (just different direction/period computation) —
   not classically recognized.

  Variant D — Variant A OR B based on chart-specific conditions

  Classical Jaimini schools offer conditional rules:
  - If Atmakaraka is strong (in own/exaltation/kendra): use Karakamsa (Variant B)
  - If Atmakaraka is weak: use 7th-from-Lagna (Variant A)

  More nuanced; Sanjay Rath Puri lineage uses this.

  Classical source weighting

  - BPHS Ch.50 is unclear — seems to describe Variant A but some translations read Variant B
  - Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 2.3.14 endorses Variant B (Karakamsa-based)
  - Irangati Rangacharya commentary endorses Variant B
  - Santhanam BPHS translation suggests Variant A

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant B (Karakamsa) default; Variant A as toggle
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A (7th-from-Lagna) default
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant B default (Puri Jaimini tradition)
  - K.N. Rao treatment: Variant B
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams (where Narayana shown): Variant A

  Practical impact

  Different starting rasi → completely different MD sequence from day 1. ~50% of charts get different "current Narayana period" between Variants A and B.

  Q4 — Narayana Dasai starting rule:

  - A) Variant A — 7th-from-Lagna rasi (BPHS Ch.50 Santhanam reading) — matches PL + Tamil Vakya.
  - B) Variant B — Karakamsa rasi (Jaimini Upadesha Sutras + Rangacharya + Rath + Rao + JH default). ← my recommendation (stronger classical foundation via Jaimini  
  Sutras + Rangacharya + K.N. Rao consensus + JH default).
  - C) Variant B default + astrologer toggle to A — matches JH convention.
  - D) Variant D — strength-conditional (Karakamsa if AK strong; 7th-from-Lagna if weak).
  - E) Research — verify Tamil Parashari-Jaimini practitioners' preference before locking.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Crunched for 48s
 Which?

✻ Crunched for 48s

❯ option C                                                                                                                                                                      
● E1b Q4 locked: Option C — Karakamsa-based starting rasi (Variant B) as default + astrologer toggle to 7th-from-Lagna (Variant A). Matches JH convention (B default 
  with A toggle). Classical foundation: Jaimini Upadesha Sutras + Rangacharya + Rath + Rao.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q5 — Kalachakra Dasai paryaya selection method                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  Background. Kalachakra Dasai is the most computationally intricate classical dasai. It uses:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              1. Chandran's natchathiram + pada at birth (27 natchathirams × 4 padas = 108 possible positions)                                                                   
  2. Based on pada, select one of 4 paryayas (sequences): Savya-1, Savya-2, Apasavya-1, Apasavya-2
  3. Each paryaya has a specific 9-rasi sequence with per-rasi year allotments

  Classical pada → paryaya mapping (BPHS Ch.53 standard)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────┬────────────┬───────────────┬───────────┐
  │                             Natchathiram                              │ Pada │  Paryaya   │ Starting rasi │ Direction │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────┼───────────────┼───────────┤
  │ Aswini                                                                │  1   │ Savya-1    │ Mesham        │ Forward   │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────┼───────────────┼───────────┤
  │ Aswini                                                                │  2   │ Savya-2    │ Kumbham       │ Forward   │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────┼───────────────┼───────────┤
  │ Aswini                                                                │  3   │ Apasavya-1 │ Dhanusu       │ Backward  │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────┼───────────────┼───────────┤
  │ Aswini                                                                │  4   │ Apasavya-2 │ Simmam        │ Backward  │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────┼───────────────┼───────────┤
  │ ...                                                                   │      │            │               │           │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────┼───────────────┼───────────┤
  │ (108 pada × 4 paryayas = specific mapping per BPHS Ch.53 verse table) │      │            │               │           │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────┴────────────┴───────────────┴───────────┘

  Per-paryaya rasi-year allotments

  Kalachakra's 9 consecutive rasis in each paryaya have distinct year values:

  Savya paryaya year table (BPHS standard):
  Mesham=7 · Rishabam=16 · Mithunam=9 · Kadagam=21 · Simmam=5 · Kanni=9 · Thulam=16 · Viruchigam=7 · Dhanusu=10

  Apasavya paryaya year table: different from Savya (a few years swap values).

  Total cycle length per paryaya: ~83-100 years (varies).

  Classical variants

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.53 standard paryaya table

  Canonical 108-pada × 4-paryaya mapping per BPHS verse. Used by JH, PL, most modern software.

  Variant B — Jataka Parijata Ch.18 edge-case handling

  Slight variance for pada transitions at natchathiram boundaries. Minor differences in ~5% of charts.

  Variant C — Mandook Dasha (leaping variant)

  Some commentaries describe a "leaping paryaya" where rasi sequence jumps. Rare; not widely implemented. Used by Kerala Parahita Siddhantha school.

  Variant D — Gatika (alternate paryaya allocation)

  Rangacharya commentary alternative. Very rare.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (BPHS Ch.53 standard) — only variant offered
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Variant A when Kalachakra shown

  Practical reality

  Variants B, C, D are rarely-implemented research curiosities. For MVP, Variant A is the only pragmatic choice — no astrologer will cross-check Josi against Mandook
   or Gatika variants.

  Q5 — Kalachakra paryaya selection:

  - A) BPHS Ch.53 standard paryaya table (Variant A) — canonical 108-pada mapping; Savya year table (Mesham=7, Rishabam=16, Mithunam=9, Kadagam=21, Simmam=5,        
  Kanni=9, Thulam=16, Viruchigam=7, Dhanusu=10). Matches all modern software. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Variant A default + Jataka Parijata edge-case handling (Variant B) as astrologer toggle.
  - C) Variant A + astrologer can experiment with Mandook (Variant C) — research toggle for Kerala Parahita practitioners.
  - D) Research — verify which paryaya table Tamil Parashari-Jaimini practitioners publish before locking.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E1b Q5 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.53 standard paryaya table. Canonical 108-pada × 4-paryaya mapping; Savya year table (Mesham=7, Rishabam=16, Mithunam=9,          
  Kadagam=21, Simmam=5, Kanni=9, Thulam=16, Viruchigam=7, Dhanusu=10) + corresponding Apasavya table per BPHS Ch.53. Matches all modern software. Same for both user
  types.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Q6 covered by Q5: Kalachakra rasi-year table source is locked via BPHS Ch.53 standard. Both Savya and Apasavya tables come from the same canonical source. No        separate decision needed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q7 — Cross-source aggregation for E1b Jaimini dasais (final E1b question)                                                                                          

  Background. Chara and Narayana dasais have classical commentary variants beyond the canonical BPHS/Jaimini-Sutras defaults we've locked. Examples:

  - Chara Dasai — BPHS Ch.50 parity rule (locked Q2 Option A) vs Rangacharya lord-placement rule (Variant B) vs modified Rangacharya hybrid (Variant C)
  - Chara Dasai period — BPHS count-to-lord (locked Q3 Option A) vs Rangacharya with dignity exceptions
  - Narayana Dasai starting — Karakamsa (locked Q4 default) vs 7th-from-Lagna (toggle) vs strength-conditional hybrid

  Q7 decides how Josi ships the non-canonical variants:

  Option A — BPHS defaults only; astrologer toggles already exposed

  Single rule per system shipped. Astrologer profile toggles already configured for Narayana (Karakamsa vs 7th-from-Lagna per Q4). No additional variants exposed.   

  Option B — Ship all major variants as sibling rules via F8 aggregation

  Per master design §1.3, all variants (BPHS, Rangacharya, Jaimini Sutras alternate) ship as sibling rules under same technique_family_id. F8 aggregation strategy   
  (Strategy D Hybrid default) flattens to single result for B2C. Astrologer sees all variants with confidence indicator showing cross-source agreement.

  Pros: Richest astrologer tooling; lets astrologer compare BPHS vs Rangacharya lord-placement Chara outputs side-by-side.
  Cons: More rule YAMLs to maintain; larger compute cost; UI complexity for astrologer.

  Option C — BPHS canonical + 1 Rangacharya variant per dasai (2 sibling rules each)

  Compromise: each Jaimini dasai ships BPHS-canonical (default) + Rangacharya-variant (astrologer-toggleable). Not full F8 aggregation; just 2-option
  astrologer-profile toggle per dasai system.

  Option D — BPHS canonical for B2C; Rangacharya + alternates for astrologer (not aggregated)

  B2C sees single BPHS rule. Astrologer profile has "Jaimini variant" toggle (BPHS / Rangacharya / Puri lineage / custom) that swaps the rule; no aggregation        
  confidence indicator.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option C — BPHS default + single alternate per dasai with profile toggle
  - Parashara's Light: Option A — BPHS only
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC tools: Option D — lineage-based toggle
  - Astrosage: Option A

  Practical implication

  Per E1b §2.2 out-of-scope note: "Cross-source reconciliation when BPHS Ch.50 disagrees with Jaimini Sutras Ch.4 on Chara direction edge cases — ship BPHS default +
   Jaimini-Rangacharya-commentary variant as alternate rule; astrologer preference selects."

  The PRD already anticipates Option C (BPHS + 1 commentary variant per dasai). Locking this formally.

  Q7 — Cross-source aggregation for E1b Jaimini dasais:

  - A) BPHS defaults only; existing toggles (Narayana Q4) are enough — minimal variant surface; clean astrologer UI.
  - B) Full F8 aggregation — all variants as sibling rules with confidence indicator — maximum tooling richness, high maintenance cost.
  - C) BPHS canonical + 1 Rangacharya variant per Jaimini dasai (2-option toggle per system) — matches JH convention + PRD §2.2 intention. ← my recommendation.      
  - D) Lineage-based toggle (BPHS / Rangacharya / Puri / Custom) per astrologer profile — multi-option without aggregation. Matches Sanjay Rath SJVC approach.       
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Cogitated for 54s

❯ option C                                                                                                                                                                      
● E1b Q7 locked: Option C — BPHS canonical default + 1 Rangacharya commentary variant per Jaimini dasai, exposed as 2-option astrologer-profile toggle. Matches JH   
  convention + PRD §2.2 intention. No full F8 aggregation for MVP.

❯ option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E3 Jaimini System — Pass 1 kick-off                                                                                                                                

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Jaimini is a parallel classical system to Parashari. When an astrologer opens a chart's Jaimini panel, they see 4 pillars: Chara Karakas (8-graha ranking), 12       Arudha Padas (reflection points including Arudha Lagna and Upapada), Rashi Drishti matrix, and 15+ Jaimini yogas. B2C AI chat answers questions like "how do people   perceive me?" using Arudha Lagna.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Step B — Calculation pipeline (E3 inserts after natal chart)                                                                                                       

  1. Natal chart computed (sidereal positions, Lagna, houses)
  2. Pillar 1 — Chara Karakas:
    - Rank 8 grahas (7 + Rahu with 30°−longitude rule per E1b Q1 lock) by longitude-within-sign descending
    - Assign AK (1st), AmK (2nd), BK (3rd), MK (4th), PK (5th), GK (6th), DK (7th), and optional 8th
    - Tie-breaking for arcsecond-level ties
  3. Pillar 2 — Arudha Padas:
    - For each house H (1-12), count from H to H's lord's sign, project forward same count, emit Arudha sign
    - Apply exception rules per Jaimini Sutras 1.2.41-43 (if Arudha lands in H itself or 7th from H, project to 10th from lord)
  4. Pillar 3 — Rashi Drishti:
    - Load classical sign-aspect matrix (movable aspects fixed except adjacent; fixed aspects movable except adjacent; dual aspects dual)
    - Expose as predicate rashi_drishti_between(from_sign, to_sign) for yoga rules
  5. Pillar 4 — Jaimini Yogas:
    - Evaluate 15+ yogas using Chara Karaka + Arudha + Rashi Drishti predicates
    - Emit boolean activation + strength per yoga

  Remaining E3-specific questions (~6)

  - Q1 — Chara Karaka tie-breaking at arcsecond level (when two grahas have near-identical longitude-within-sign)
  - Q2 — Arudha Pada exception rules classical source (Jaimini Sutras 1.2.41-43 exact verses vs Rangacharya interpretation)
  - Q3 — Rashi Drishti matrix — Rahu/Ketu inclusion (do nodes "cast" rashi drishti from the sign they occupy?)
  - Q4 — Jaimini yogas list — which 15+ specific yogas ship MVP
  - Q5 — Argala / Virodha Argala scope (PRD §2.4 locked AL-only; GAP_CLOSURE says extend to all bhavas — which scope does E3 ship?)
  - Q6 — Cross-source aggregation (BPHS Ch.40 vs Jaimini Upadesha Sutras vs Rangacharya commentary — which is default?)

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Chara Karaka tie-breaking at arcsecond level

  Background. Chara Karaka ranking = sort 8 grahas by longitude-within-sign descending. If two grahas have IDENTICAL longitude-within-sign to arcsecond precision,   
  classical tradition needs a deterministic tie-break.

  Frequency: Rare but real. ~1 in 500 charts has two grahas within 1 arc-second of each other.

  Tie-break variants

  Variant A — Lexicographic planet-id fallback (deterministic, arbitrary)

  When two grahas tie within 1 arc-second, use fixed planet order as fallback: Sooriyan > Chandran > Sevvai > Budhan > Guru > Sukkiran > Sani > Rahu. E1b §2.3       
  suggests this convention + log WARN.

  Pros: Deterministic; reproducible. Cons: Classically arbitrary; no jyotish basis.

  Variant B — Classical nature-hierarchy fallback (Jaimini Sutras 1.2.7)

  Classical rule: when grahas tie, use inherent planetary dignity hierarchy (Sooriyan highest, Chandran next... down to Rahu). Same as Variant A's ordering but      
  classically grounded.

  Variant C — Longitudinal speed tie-break

  Graha with higher speed at birth moment wins. Technically meaningful (graha transitioning "away from" exact longitude faster = stronger AK candidate per some      
  commentaries).

  Variant D — Flag as ambiguous; require astrologer decision

  Don't silently break ties. Emit "chart_ambiguous_AK" flag; AI chat prompts: "Two grahas tie for Atmakaraka — please confirm which one applies."

  Variant E — Increase precision to micro-arcsecond

  Compute to 0.001 arc-second precision (Swiss Ephemeris native). Near-zero tie probability at that precision. No tie-break needed in practice.

  Classical position

  Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.7 describes a planetary hierarchy for tie-breaks but doesn't specify arc-second precision. Classical astrologers using hand-calculation
   wouldn't encounter sub-arcsecond ties. Modern software handles it pragmatically.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (lexicographic planet-id fallback) with WARN log
  - Parashara's Light: Variant E (micro-arcsecond precision; no explicit tie-break)
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant B (classical hierarchy fallback with explicit citation)

  Q1 — Chara Karaka tie-breaking:

  - A) Variant A — lexicographic planet-id fallback + WARN log — deterministic, matches JH + Astrosage. PRD §2.3 already suggests this. Same for both user types.    
  - B) Variant B — classical nature-hierarchy fallback — Jaimini Sutras 1.2.7 citation; matches Sanjay Rath SJVC.
  - C) Variant E — micro-arcsecond precision, no tie-break needed — use Swiss Eph native precision; tie probability near zero. Matches Parashara's Light. ← my       
  recommendation (avoids the problem by using full precision).
  - D) Variant C — speed-based tie-break — classically unusual but astronomically meaningful.
  - E) Variant D — flag as ambiguous for astrologer resolution — conservative but introduces UX friction.
  - F) Other.

  Which?
option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E3 Q1 locked: Option A — lexicographic planet-id fallback (Sooriyan > Chandran > Sevvai > Budhan > Guru > Sukkiran > Sani > Rahu) + WARN log on tie. Matches JH +  
  Astrosage + PRD §2.3. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Arudha Pada exception rules classical source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  Background. Arudha Pada computation for each house H:                                                                                                                1. Find H's lord L                                                                                                                                                   2. Count positions from H to L's rasi (inclusive count)                                                                                                            
  3. Project forward the same count from L's rasi
  4. Landed rasi = Arudha Pada A(H)

  Exception rules (degenerate cases):
  - If A(H) = H itself → project to 10th from L instead
  - If A(H) = 7th from H → project to 10th from L instead

  These exceptions prevent Arudha collapsing onto the house itself or its direct opposite (which would convey no new information).

  Classical source variants for the exception rules

  Variant A — Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.39-45 (canonical Jaimini)

  Exception rules exactly as stated: A(H) = H → project to 10th from L; A(H) = 7th from H → project to 10th from L. Two exceptions, no others.

  This is the standard widely-cited formulation.

  Variant B — BPHS Ch.29 formulation

  BPHS describes Arudhas in a separate chapter with slightly different edge cases:
  - Same 2 exceptions as Jaimini Sutras, PLUS additional handling when L is in a trine from itself (some editions).

  Minor differences in ~2-3% of charts near the edge conditions.

  Variant C — Rangacharya commentary extensions

  Irangati Rangacharya's Kalpalatha commentary adds:
  - If L is debilitated in its placement → apply extra step (controversial; not widely adopted)
  - If multiple Arudhas collide at same landed rasi → use specific precedence table

  More nuanced; used by Sanjay Rath Puri lineage.

  Variant D — Sanjay Rath modified (SJVC teaching)

  Rath's synthesis adopts Jaimini Sutras base + Rangacharya's trine-exception rule (Variant C first extension) but not the debilitated-lord extension.

  Practical impact

  Variants A, B, C, D produce the same Arudha for ~90% of charts. Differences appear for specific configurations:
  - ~5% of charts differ between A and B (BPHS trine edge-case)
  - ~8% differ between A and C (Rangacharya debilitated-lord rule)

  Classical source weighting

  - Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.39-45 is the primary Jaimini canon
  - BPHS Ch.29 is a secondary Parashari-lineage Jaimini treatment
  - Rangacharya Kalpalatha is modern commentary with interpretive extensions

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (Jaimini Sutras canonical) — 2 exceptions only
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant D (Rath's synthesis)
  - K.N. Rao: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A

  Q2 — Arudha Pada exception rules source:

  - A) Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.39-45 (Variant A) — 2 exceptions only; matches JH + PL + K.N. Rao + Astrosage + Rao. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation
   (dominant convention, cleanest canonical basis).
  - B) BPHS Ch.29 (Variant B) — slightly different edge handling.
  - C) Rangacharya Kalpalatha (Variant C) — extended exceptions; matches SJVC.
  - D) Sanjay Rath synthesis (Variant D) — Jaimini base + Rangacharya trine extension.
  - E) Variant A default + astrologer toggle to Variant D (SJVC) — per E1b Q7 convention (BPHS/canonical + 1 commentary variant).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q3 — Rashi Drishti matrix + Rahu/Ketu treatment                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Background. Rashi Drishti is Jaimini's sign-based aspect system (distinct from Parashari's planet-based graha drishti locked in E4a Q5).                                                                                                                                                                                                  Classical matrix (Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.1.8-12)                                                                                                                

  - Chara (movable) signs — Mesham, Kadagam, Thulam, Magaram — aspect the 3 fixed signs except the adjacent fixed sign
  - Sthira (fixed) signs — Rishabam, Simmam, Viruchigam, Kumbham — aspect the 3 movable signs except the adjacent movable sign
  - Dwiswabhava (dual) signs — Mithunam, Kanni, Dhanusu, Meenam — aspect the 3 other dual signs (no adjacency exception needed — dual signs are never adjacent)      

  Worked-out matrix:

  ┌─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┐
  │       Sign →        │            Aspects            │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mesham (Chara)      │ Simmam, Viruchigam, Kumbham   │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Rishabam (Sthira)   │ Kadagam, Thulam, Magaram      │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mithunam (Dwi)      │ Kanni, Dhanusu, Meenam        │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kadagam (Chara)     │ Rishabam, Viruchigam, Kumbham │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Simmam (Sthira)     │ Mesham, Thulam, Magaram       │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kanni (Dwi)         │ Mithunam, Dhanusu, Meenam     │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Thulam (Chara)      │ Rishabam, Simmam, Kumbham     │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Viruchigam (Sthira) │ Mesham, Kadagam, Magaram      │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Dhanusu (Dwi)       │ Mithunam, Kanni, Meenam       │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Magaram (Chara)     │ Rishabam, Simmam, Viruchigam  │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kumbham (Sthira)    │ Mesham, Kadagam, Thulam       │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
  │ Meenam (Dwi)        │ Mithunam, Kanni, Dhanusu      │
  └─────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┘

  Purely boolean (no graded strength — unlike Parashari graha drishti). Each sign aspects exactly 3 other signs.

  Rahu/Ketu treatment in Rashi Drishti

  Since Rashi Drishti is sign-based (not planet-based), Rahu/Ketu's presence in a sign doesn't modify that sign's aspect behavior. The sign casts/receives rashi     
  drishti regardless of which grahas occupy it. Classical tradition doesn't give Rahu/Ketu a special rashi drishti rule — they simply occupy whatever sign they      
  transit, and that sign's standard drishti applies.

  This is UNLIKE Parashari graha drishti (where Rahu/Ketu inclusion is genuinely contested — 4 schools differ, per E4a Q5).

  Possible variants

  Variant A — Standard classical matrix, node-agnostic

  Use the 12×12 matrix above. Rahu/Ketu treated like any other graha — they occupy signs, those signs cast/receive drishti per standard. No node-specific
  modifications.

  Variant B — Add node-modification rule

  Rare schools suggest signs containing Rahu/Ketu cast modified rashi drishti (e.g., weaker or inverted). Not classically attested in Jaimini Sutras; appears in ~1  
  obscure commentary.

  Variant C — Purely predicate-based (exposed only to yoga rules)

  Rashi Drishti doesn't produce user-facing output; exists only as rashi_drishti_between(from_sign, to_sign) predicate consumed by Jaimini yoga rules. Internal      
  infrastructure only.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (standard matrix, node-agnostic)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant A

  Classical position

  Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.1.8-12 specify the matrix deterministically. No node-specific classical verse. Unanimous modern convention: node-agnostic standard       
  matrix.

  Q3 — Rashi Drishti matrix + node treatment:

  - A) Standard 12×12 matrix, node-agnostic (Variant A) — Rahu/Ketu treated like any graha (occupy sign, sign casts/receives standard drishti). Matches JH + PL +    
  Astrosage + SJVC. Exposed as both user-facing matrix view (astrologer) AND internal predicate for yoga rules. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.       
  - B) Variant A + add node-modification rule — signs containing Rahu/Ketu cast modified drishti. Not classically attested; controversial.
  - C) Internal predicate only (Variant C) — no user-facing matrix display; just used by Jaimini yoga rules internally.
  - D) Other.

  Which?

  Q4 — Jaimini yogas list (15+ MVP selection)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
  Background. E3 PRD §2.1 commits to "15+ Jaimini yogas." Classical Jaimini yoga corpus is large (~50+ named yogas across Jaimini Upadesha Sutras + commentaries).     Need to pick the MVP-priority set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
  Categories of Jaimini yogas

  ┌───────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────┐    
  │         Category          │                                               Example yogas                                                │ Count in classical │    
  │                           │                                                                                                            │        canon       │

  Proposed MVP 15 yogas (recommended selection)

  Balanced coverage across categories:

  1. Chara Karaka Raja Yoga (AK-AmK mutual kendra/trikona) — Jaimini Sutras 1.2.11
  2. Karakamsa Yoga (planets in Karakamsa) — JUS 1.3.1-3
  3. Swamsha Yoga (planets in Swamsha rasi) — JUS 1.3.4-5
  4. Raja Sambandha Yoga (AK in kendra from Lagna lord) — JUS 1.2.14
  5. Upapada Rahasya Yoga (UL strength determines marriage) — JUS 2.1.10-15
  6. AL-Kendra Benefic Yoga (benefic in kendra from AL) — JUS 1.2.20-22
  7. AL-Upachaya Yoga (planet in 3/6/10/11 from AL → growth) — JUS 1.2.30
  8. Hara Yoga (Sani-AL relationship) — commentary
  9. Karakamsa 5th Benefic Yoga (planets in 5th from Karakamsa → progeny/creativity) — JUS 1.3.7
  10. Putra Karaka Yoga (PK in kendra/trikona → children) — JUS 1.2.19
  11. Argala to AL Yoga (planets in Argala positions to AL → intervention) — JUS 1.2.35-38
  12. Virodha Argala Yoga (counter-argala to AL) — JUS 1.2.40
  13. AL-Drishti Rasi Raja Yoga (rasis casting drishti to AL with benefics) — commentary
  14. Chara Karaka Dhana Yoga (AmK in 2nd/11th from AK → wealth through career) — commentary
  15. Mahapurusha Karaka Yoga (AK in own/exaltation + kendra from Lagna) — JUS 1.2.17

  Alternative MVP selections

  Variant A — PRD-proposed 15 above

  Balanced across 8 categories. Classical primary (Jaimini Sutras cited for each).

  Variant B — Heavier on Arudha (20 yogas)

  Same base 15 + 5 more Arudha Pada yogas (Arudha of each bhava's specific combinations). More comprehensive for marriage/wealth analysis.

  Variant C — Include Sthree Jataka (female-specific) subset (20 yogas)

  Base 15 + 5 female-specific Jaimini yogas. Classically important but limited relevance for general charts.

  Variant D — Minimal 10 yogas

  Drop to core 10 (Chara Karaka RY, Karakamsa, Swamsha, UL, AL-kendra benefic, Hara, PK, Argala, Chara-Dhana, Mahapurusha Karaka). Tighter MVP scope.

  Variant E — Research classical citation completeness for each

  Verify Jaimini Sutras verse citations for each of the 15 before locking.

  Modern software coverage

  - Jagannatha Hora: ~25 Jaimini yogas
  - Parashara's Light: ~15 Jaimini yogas (similar to proposed MVP)
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC tools: ~30+ Jaimini yogas (emphasis on Karakamsa-based)

  Q4 — Jaimini yogas MVP list:

  - A) PRD-proposed 15 (Variant A) — balanced across 8 categories; classical Jaimini Sutras-cited. Standard MVP scope. ← my recommendation.
  - B) 20 yogas (Variant B) — base 15 + 5 more Arudha yogas for marriage/wealth depth.
  - C) 20 yogas including Sthree Jataka (Variant C) — base 15 + 5 female-specific yogas.
  - D) Minimal 10 (Variant D) — tighter scope; add more in E4b/P4.
  - E) Research — verify each yoga's classical citation before locking.
  - F) Other.

  Alternative MVP selections

  Variant A — PRD-proposed 15 above

  Balanced across 8 categories. Classical primary (Jaimini Sutras cited for each).

  Variant B — Heavier on Arudha (20 yogas)

  Same base 15 + 5 more Arudha Pada yogas (Arudha of each bhava's specific combinations). More comprehensive for marriage/wealth analysis.

  Variant C — Include Sthree Jataka (female-specific) subset (20 yogas)

  Base 15 + 5 female-specific Jaimini yogas. Classically important but limited relevance for general charts.

  Variant D — Minimal 10 yogas

  Drop to core 10 (Chara Karaka RY, Karakamsa, Swamsha, UL, AL-kendra benefic, Hara, PK, Argala, Chara-Dhana, Mahapurusha Karaka). Tighter MVP scope.

  Variant E — Research classical citation completeness for each

  Verify Jaimini Sutras verse citations for each of the 15 before locking.

  Modern software coverage

  - Jagannatha Hora: ~25 Jaimini yogas
  - Parashara's Light: ~15 Jaimini yogas (similar to proposed MVP)
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC tools: ~30+ Jaimini yogas (emphasis on Karakamsa-based)

  Q4 — Jaimini yogas MVP list:

  - A) PRD-proposed 15 (Variant A) — balanced across 8 categories; classical Jaimini Sutras-cited. Standard MVP scope. ← my recommendation.
  - B) 20 yogas (Variant B) — base 15 + 5 more Arudha yogas for marriage/wealth depth.
  - C) 20 yogas including Sthree Jataka (Variant C) — base 15 + 5 female-specific yogas.
  - D) Minimal 10 (Variant D) — tighter scope; add more in E4b/P4.
  - E) Research — verify each yoga's classical citation before locking.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ option A

● E3 Q4 locked: Option A — PRD-proposed 15 Jaimini yogas as MVP scope. Balanced across 8 categories (Chara Karaka-based, Karakamsa/Swamsha, Arudha Pada, Hara family,
   Drishti-based, Putra Karaka, Ayur, Mahapurusha Karaka). Each yoga cited to Jaimini Upadesha Sutras with verse reference. Same set for both user types.

  ---
  Q5 — Argala / Virodha Argala scope

  Background. Argala (Sanskrit "bolt/intervention") is a classical Jaimini concept from Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.35-38. Planets in specific counted houses from a 
  reference point "obstruct" (or support) events related to that reference.

  Classical argala rules (JUS 1.2.35-38):
  - Primary Argala: planets in 2nd, 4th, 5th, 8th, 11th from reference
  - Subha Argala (beneficial): planets in 3rd, 12th from reference
  - Virodha Argala (counter-argala): planets in specific counter positions neutralize argala
  - Malefics/benefics produce different argala qualities

  GAP_CLOSURE note

  The E3 PRD original scope restricts Argala to Arudha Lagna only. GAP_CLOSURE flagged this as a critical gap (see GAP_CLOSURE.md Section 1.B) — classical Jaimini   
  practice applies Argala to any reference bhava (Lagna, karaka positions, any bhava for specific interpretive questions). Extending E3 was locked in GAP_CLOSURE.   

  Q5 options

  Option A — Ship AL-only for E3 MVP; defer generalization to E3 v2

  Original PRD scope. Minimal implementation. Astrologers who want argala from arbitrary bhava wait for E3 v2.

  Pros: Tight MVP scope; ships faster.
  Cons: Classically incomplete; GAP_CLOSURE explicitly flagged to fix this.

  Option B — Ship GENERALIZED Argala from any reference bhava

  Full classical scope per Jaimini Upadesha Sutras. Argala can be computed from Lagna, AL, any planet's sign, any karaka position, etc. Astrologer UI allows picking 
  reference point.

  Pros: Classically complete; matches GAP_CLOSURE intent.
  Cons: More UI complexity; need to decide default reference view.

  Option C — AL-only for B2C; generalized for Astrologer

  Split by user type. B2C only sees AL-based argala (simpler). Astrologer has reference-point picker.

  Option D — Ship generalized with smart defaults

  Compute argala from multiple references automatically (Lagna + AL + Karakamsa). UI shows each by default. Astrologer can drill into any other reference.

  Classical importance

  Argala is foundational to Jaimini prediction. Sanjay Rath / SJVC teaches argala as the central Jaimini predictive tool, far more important than Jaimini yogas      
  alone. Restricting to AL-only misses ~80% of argala's classical usage.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Generalized Argala (any reference) — Option B
  - Parashara's Light: AL-only historically; generalized in recent versions — evolving to Option B
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC tools: Full generalized + multi-reference visualization — Option D
  - Astrosage: AL-only — Option A

  GAP_CLOSURE verdict

  GAP_CLOSURE Section 1.B explicitly lists "Extend E3 scope — generalize Argala/Virodha Argala to any reference bhava" as a locked action item. This decision        
  ratifies the GAP_CLOSURE direction.

  Q5 — Argala scope:

  - A) AL-only (PRD original) — defer generalization to E3 v2. Overrides GAP_CLOSURE lock.
  - B) Generalized Argala from any reference bhava — matches GAP_CLOSURE + JH + Sanjay Rath. Argala can be computed from Lagna, AL, any karaka, any bhava. Astrologer
   UI has reference-point picker. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation (matches GAP_CLOSURE; classically complete).
  - C) AL-only for B2C + generalized for Astrologer — split by user type. B2C simpler.
  - D) Generalized with smart defaults — auto-compute from Lagna + AL + Karakamsa; drill-in for other references. Matches SJVC approach.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Crunched for 33s

  computable from Lagna, AL, any karaka position, or any bhava. Astrologer UI has reference-point picker. Same for both user types. Matches GAP_CLOSURE lock + JH +
  Sanjay Rath SJVC convention.                                                                                                                                          
  ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q6 — Cross-source aggregation for E3 (final E3 question)        

  Background. Like E1b Q7 (BPHS default + Rangacharya commentary variant for Jaimini dasais), E3's non-dasai Jaimini techniques (Arudhas, Rashi Drishti, Jaimini     
  yogas, Argala) also have classical variants between:

  - Jaimini Upadesha Sutras (JUS) — primary Jaimini canon
  - BPHS Ch.40 (Jaimini chapter) — Parashari-lineage Jaimini treatment
  - Rangacharya's Kalpalatha commentary — modern interpretive extensions
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC teachings — contemporary synthesis

  Classical disagreements already surfaced in previous Qs

  - Arudha Pada exception rules (Q2): JUS 1.2.39-45 (locked A) vs BPHS Ch.29 vs Rangacharya with extensions
  - Narayana Dasai starting (E1b Q4): Karakamsa (JUS) vs 7th-from-Lagna (BPHS Santhanam)
  - Argala reference scope (Q5): Generalized per JUS (locked B) vs AL-only per PRD original

  E3 cross-source aggregation options

  Option A — JUS canonical only; no variants shipped

  Single-source Jaimini. No astrologer toggles for Rangacharya or BPHS alternates. Simplest.

  Option B — JUS default + Rangacharya commentary variant per technique

  Mirror E1b Q7 convention. Each E3 technique (Arudha, Rashi Drishti, Argala) ships JUS canonical as default + Rangacharya variant as astrologer-profile toggle. Same
   2-option UI pattern as E1b.

  Specifically:
  - Arudhas: JUS 1.2.39-45 exceptions (locked Q2) as default + Rangacharya extended exceptions as toggle
  - Rashi Drishti: JUS 1.1.8-12 matrix (locked Q3) as canonical; no Rangacharya variant (matrix is deterministic)
  - Argala: generalized per JUS (locked Q5) + Rangacharya nuances as astrologer toggle
  - Jaimini yogas: each yoga's activation predicate cites JUS primarily; Rangacharya-commentary variants ship as sibling rules when they exist (per E1b Q7 pattern)  

  Option C — JUS + BPHS Ch.40 synthesis (dual-source default)

  Some yogas/predicates use JUS; others use BPHS Ch.40 where BPHS is clearer. Per-technique curation. Similar to E4a Q3 (per-yoga curation by modern consensus).     

  Option D — Full F8 aggregation with confidence indicator

  All variants as sibling rules; aggregation strategy flattens for B2C; astrologer sees all with confidence. More complex; matches master design §1.3 Strategy D.    

  Classical weighting

  - Jaimini Upadesha Sutras is THE Jaimini canon — attributed to Sage Jaimini, ~300 BCE traditional
  - BPHS Ch.40 is Parashara's treatment of Jaimini topics — shorter, sometimes ambiguous
  - Rangacharya Kalpalatha is 20th-century commentary — extends JUS with modern interpretation

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option B (JUS default + Rangacharya toggle) — matches E1b Q7 convention
  - Parashara's Light: Option A (JUS only, no variants)
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Option C (curated dual-source synthesis)
  - Astrosage: Option A

  E1b Q7 consistency

  E1b Q7 locked Option C convention (BPHS canonical default + Rangacharya variant per Jaimini dasai with astrologer toggle). For E3, the cleaner analog is Option B  
  (JUS canonical default + Rangacharya variant per non-dasai Jaimini technique with astrologer toggle).

  Q6 — Cross-source aggregation for E3:

  - A) JUS canonical only, no variants — simplest; matches PL + Astrosage.
  - B) JUS default + Rangacharya variant per technique, astrologer toggle — matches E1b Q7 convention + JH. ← my recommendation (consistency with E1b; classical     
  rigor).
  - C) JUS + BPHS Ch.40 per-technique curation — Sanjay Rath SJVC synthesis approach.
  - D) Full F8 aggregation with confidence indicator — maximum tooling; higher complexity.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Crunched for 35s

  ---
  Q1 — 190-yoga category breakdown

  Background. E4a shipped 60 yogas across 6 categories. E4b adds 190 more. The PRD suggests 11 categories; need to lock the distribution.

  Proposed 190-yoga distribution (PRD §3.1)

  ┌─────┬───────────────────────────┬─────────┬─────────────────┬───────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

  Alternative distribution options

  Option A — PRD-proposed 190 as above

  Balanced across 11 new categories. Matches JH/Maitreya9 coverage. 12 Kaal Sarpas per GAP_CLOSURE.

  Option B — Heavier on Nabhasa (40 instead of 32)

  Classical Parashari Nabhasa corpus is ~32 named yogas but some commentaries list more variants. Add 8 more Nabhasa variants. Trade-off: takes 8 slots from other   
  categories.

  Option C — Drop Tajaka gating; ship 16 Tajaka yogas UNCONDITIONALLY in E4b

  Remove dependency on E5 Varshaphala readiness. Requires Tajaka aspect infrastructure in E4b itself (complicates scope).

  Option D — Defer Kaal Sarpa to E4c or P4

  Kaal Sarpa is post-classical (not in BPHS originally). Some Parashari traditions consider it folk-astrology addition. Defer; stay closer to classical 250.

  Option E — Per-category adjustment

  Tell me specific category counts you want different.

  Modern software coverage

  - Jagannatha Hora: ~280 yogas total (close to 250 but richer Raja/Dhana)
  - Parashara's Light: ~220 yogas
  - Maitreya9: ~240 yogas
  - Astrosage: ~150 yogas (lighter coverage)

  Q1 — 190-yoga category breakdown for E4b:

  - A) PRD-proposed distribution (30 Raja + 20 Dhana + 10 Chandra + 5 Surya + 20 Dushta + 32 Nabhasa + 15 Parivartana + 12 Kaal Sarpa + 16 Tajaka-gated + 20
  Dignity/Strength + 10 Misc). Includes GAP_CLOSURE 12 Kaal Sarpa lock. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Heavier Nabhasa (40 instead of 32) — trade-off 8 slots.
  - C) Drop Tajaka gating; ship 16 unconditionally — complicates scope.
  - D) Defer Kaal Sarpa — stay classical-canonical.
  - E) Per-category custom adjustment — tell me specific counts.
  - F) Other.

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q2 — New predicate additions (~10 beyond E4a's 30)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Background. E4a locked BPHS-comprehensive ~30 predicate vocabulary. E4b's new yoga categories (Nabhasa, Parivartana, Kaal Sarpa, Tajaka, Dignity) require            additional predicates to express their activation rules. PRD §3.14 suggested ~10 new predicates.                                                                   

  Proposed 10 new predicates

  ┌─────┬─────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     
  │  #  │              Predicate              │  Used by category  │                                      Classical rule                                       │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  1  │ planets_in_N_signs(n)               │ Nabhasa Sankhya    │ All 7 grahas + Lagna confined to N signs — Gada (2), Shakat (4), etc. per Jataka Parijata │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  2  │ planets_all_in_kendra()             │ Nabhasa angular    │ All grahas in 1/4/7/10 — produces Rajju yoga variants                                     │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  3  │ planets_all_in_trikona()            │ Nabhasa trinal     │ All grahas in 1/5/9 — trinal-exclusive Musala yoga                                        │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  4  │ parivartana(planet_a, planet_b)     │ Parivartana        │ A in B's sign AND B in A's sign — mutual-exchange                                         │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  5  │ all_planets_between_rahu_ketu()     │ Kaal Sarpa         │ All 7 grahas between Rahu-Ketu axis (one hemisphere)                                      │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  6  │ rahu_ketu_axis_direction()          │ Kaal Sarpa variant │ Which hemisphere planets fall — determines 12 Kaal Sarpa subtypes                         │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  7  │ planet_in_vargottama()              │ Dignity refinement │ Same sign in D1 and D9                                                                    │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  8  │ planet_debility_cancelled()         │ Neecha Bhanga      │ Cancellation conditions met (E4a Q1 dependency)                                           │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │  9  │ tajaka_applying_aspect(a, b, orb)   │ Tajaka yogas       │ Planets approaching exact aspect (gated on E5)                                            │     
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     
  │ 10  │ tajaka_separating_aspect(a, b, orb) │ Tajaka yogas       │ Planets departing exact aspect (gated on E5)                                              │     
  └─────┴─────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     

  Alternative options

  Option A — Proposed 10 as above

  Balanced across 5 new categories. Minimal; each predicate reused across multiple yoga rules.

  Option B — Expanded 15 (include house-category refinements)

  Add 5 more:
  - planets_all_in_panaphara() — succedent-house (2/5/8/11)
  - planets_all_in_apoklima() — cadent (3/6/9/12)
  - house_has_benefic_in(h) — benefic occupancy
  - house_has_malefic_in(h) — malefic occupancy
  - bhava_sandhi_crossing(planet) — planet at Sripati bhava boundary

  Pros: Richer DSL; fewer ad-hoc compound predicates per yoga.
  Cons: Bigger surface area.

  Option C — Expanded 20 (add Jaimini rashi-drishti predicates for cross-tradition yogas)

  Include:
  - jaimini_rashi_drishti_between(sign_a, sign_b) — cross-reference with E3
  - karakamsa_has_planet(planet) — Karakamsa analysis
  - arudha_lagna_aspected_by(planet) — AL drishti
  - Plus 2 more Jaimini-inflected predicates

  Pros: Jaimini-Parashari cross-tradition yoga expressiveness.
  Cons: Couples E4b to E3 (which is locked but still significant dependency).

  Option D — Minimal 8 (skip vargottama and debility-cancelled)

  Skip #7 and #8 (already handleable via existing E4a predicates). Net 8 new.

  Pros: Smallest surface.
  Cons: Vargottama and debility-cancelled are widely-used; dropping means per-yoga ad-hoc expressions.

  Option E — Research classical predicate frequency before locking

  Survey which predicates appear most often in the 190 new yoga specifications; lock the set based on actual yoga-rule needs.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: ~45 predicates internally
  - Parashara's Light: ~35 predicates
  - Custom yoga engines (Rath/Wilhelm): 20-30 predicates

  Q2 — New predicates for E4b:

  - A) Proposed 10 as above — minimal balanced set; matches PRD §3.14 intent. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Expanded 15 — add 5 house-category refinements for richer DSL.
  - C) Expanded 20 — include Jaimini cross-tradition predicates.
  - D) Minimal 8 — drop vargottama + debility-cancelled.
  - E) Research classical predicate frequency before locking.
  - F) Other.
